---
title: "CPOX_Manuscript"
date: "2026-4-7"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

# =============================================================================
# CPOX Deep Mutational Scanning (DMS) Analysis — Manuscript Code
#
# This R Markdown document contains all analyses for the CPOX DMS manuscript.
# CPOX (coproporphyrinogen oxidase) is the enzyme deficient in hereditary
# coproporphyria (HCP). The experiment measures variant functional scores
# under two conditions:
#   • Baseline  — standard growth selection
#   • Metal     — growth selection in the presence of mercury (Hg)
#
# Chunks covered:
#   1.  Score Distributions
#   2.  Consensus ring map (circular visualisation)
#   3.  Population-level assessment (UK Biobank / gnomAD)
#   4.  Baseline vs. Metal map correlation
#   5.  Delta score analysis (baseline – metal)
#   6.  Functional scores vs. predicted ΔΔG (thermodynamic stability)
#   7.  Correlation with patient enzymatic activity
#   8.  Hypercomplementing variants vs. AlphaMissense
#   9.  Dunham 33-map substitution trend comparison
#   10. Amino acid substitution type analysis (FROM / TO)
#   11. Structure-based analysis (SASA / quaternary domains)
#   12. MTS-region structure analysis
#   13. Negative-charge substitution at second decarboxylation residues
#   14. Delta score impact classification (APR / Improved / Known)
#   15. Delta significance heatmap
#   16. Balanced Precision-Recall Curves (PRC)
#   17. PRC with random predictor
#   18. LLRp evidence for VUS reclassification
#   19. VUS reclassification summary
#   20. Variant validation (growth curves)
# =============================================================================


```{r, warning=FALSE, Libraries}
# Load all libraries used across the manuscript analyses
library(cowplot)
library(data.table)
library(ggnewscale)    
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggsignif)
library(ggnewscale)
library(gridExtra)
library(httr)          
library(jsonlite)     
library(mgsub)         
library(patchwork)
library(plotly)
library(purrr)
library(RcppRoll)
library(rstatix)
library(scales)
library(stringr)
library(tidyverse)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(reshape2)
library(yogiroc)       
library(readxl)       
library(zoo)        

```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Data Loading Helper}
# =============================================================================
# HELPER — read_sheet()
#
# Reads a named sheet from Manuscript_Data.xlsx, skipping the first row
# (generic info row), then coerces every column to numeric where possible.
# Character columns (e.g. hgvs_pro, disease_type) are left as-is.
# Use this instead of readxl::read_excel() directly throughout the script.
# =============================================================================

read_sheet <- function(sheet) {
  df <- readxl::read_excel(
    "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",
    sheet    = sheet,
    skip     = 1,
    na       = c("", "NA", "N/A", "#NAME?", "#VALUE!", "#REF!",
                 "#DIV/0!", "#NULL!", "#NUM!", "#N/A")
  )
  df[] <- lapply(df, function(col) {
    if (is.character(col)) {
      # Map Excel Inf strings to R Inf, then coerce
      col <- trimws(col)
      col[col %in% c("Inf", "inf", "INF")]   <- "Inf"
      col[col %in% c("-Inf", "-inf", "-INF")] <- "-Inf"
      converted <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(col))
      # Keep as character only if conversion turned non-NA values into NA
      non_na_orig      <- !is.na(col)
      non_na_converted <- !is.na(converted)
      if (all(non_na_converted[non_na_orig])) converted else col
    } else {
      col
    }
  })
  as.data.frame(df)
}
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Score Distributions}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 1 — Score Distributions
#
# Produces mirrored bar-density histograms for each map (Baseline, Metal).
# Missense density is plotted below zero; nonsense and synonymous above.
# Also reports:
#   • Median score per variant class
#   • Percentage of missense variants with score > 1.1 (hyperactive candidates)
# =============================================================================

process_maps <- function(baseline_path = NULL, metal_path = NULL) {
  
  # Inner helper: classify variants, bin into histogram, return plot data
  prepare_plot_data <- function(data, context, score_column = "score") {
    
    # Classify by HGVS notation suffix
    stop.is <- which(grepl("Ter$", data$hgvs_pro))   # nonsense: ends with 'Ter'
    syn.is  <- which(grepl("=$", data$hgvs_pro))      # synonymous: ends with '='
    miss.is <- setdiff(1:nrow(data), c(stop.is, syn.is))  # everything else = missense
    
    stp  <- na.omit(data[stop.is, score_column])
    syn  <- na.omit(data[syn.is, score_column])
    miss <- na.omit(data[miss.is, score_column])
    
    # Fraction of missense variants above 1.1 (potential gain-of-function)
    percent_miss_gt_1.1 <- if (length(miss) > 0) {
      sum(miss > 1.1) / length(miss) * 100
    } else { NA }
    
    medians <- data.frame(
      type = c("nonsense", "synonymous", "missense"),
      median = c(median(stp), median(syn), median(miss)),
      context = context
    )
    
    # Bin scores into fixed-width histogram bins (-2 to 2.5, width 0.1)
    stp_hist  <- hist(stp,  breaks = seq(-2, 2.5, 0.1), plot = FALSE)
    syn_hist  <- hist(syn,  breaks = seq(-2, 2.5, 0.1), plot = FALSE)
    miss_hist <- hist(miss, breaks = seq(-2, 2.5, 0.1), plot = FALSE)
    
    # Negate missense density for mirrored plot
    stp_df  <- data.frame(bin = stp_hist$mids,  density = stp_hist$density,  type = "nonsense")
    syn_df  <- data.frame(bin = syn_hist$mids,  density = syn_hist$density,  type = "synonymous")
    miss_df <- data.frame(bin = miss_hist$mids, density = -miss_hist$density, type = "missense")
    
    combined <- rbind(stp_df, syn_df, miss_df)
    combined$context <- context
    list(plot_data = combined, medians = medians, percent_miss_gt_1.1 = percent_miss_gt_1.1)
  }
  
  data_list    <- list()
  medians_list <- list()
  percentages  <- list()
  contexts     <- c("Baseline", "Metal")
  
  if (!is.null(baseline_path)) {
    baseline_map <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
    baseline_results <- prepare_plot_data(baseline_map, "Baseline")
    data_list[["Baseline"]]   <- baseline_results$plot_data
    medians_list[["Baseline"]] <- baseline_results$medians
    percentages[["Baseline"]]  <- baseline_results$percent_miss_gt_1.1
  }
  
  if (!is.null(metal_path)) {
    metal_map <- read_sheet("Mercury Map")
    metal_results <- prepare_plot_data(metal_map, "Metal")
    data_list[["Metal"]]   <- metal_results$plot_data
    medians_list[["Metal"]] <- metal_results$medians
    percentages[["Metal"]]  <- metal_results$percent_miss_gt_1.1
  }
  
  print("Medians for each group:")
  medians_df <- do.call(rbind, medians_list)
  print(medians_df)
  
  print("Percentage of missense variants > 1.1:")
  percentages_df <- data.frame(
    context = names(percentages),
    percent_miss_gt_1.1 = unlist(percentages)
  )
  print(percentages_df)
  
  # Plot and save a PDF for each condition
  for (context in contexts) {
    if (!is.null(data_list[[context]])) {
      plot_data <- data_list[[context]]
      p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = bin, y = density, fill = type)) +
        geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "identity",
                 width = 0.1, color = "black", alpha = 0.4) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = c(
          "nonsense"   = "#C9364E",
          "synonymous" = "#003366",
          "missense"   = "grey"
        )) +
        labs(
          title = paste("Histogram of Variant Densities:", context),
          x = "Functional Score",
          y = "Density"
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
          axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
          axis.text  = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.position = "top"
        )
      print(p)
      
      filename <- paste0("~/Desktop/", context, "_Histogram.pdf")
      ggsave(filename, plot = p, width = 8, height = 6)
    }
  }
}

process_maps(
  baseline_path = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",
  metal_path    = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx"
)
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Consensus}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 2 — Consensus Circular Map
#
# Builds a polar (circular) visualisation of the full CPOX protein showing:
#   • Outer ring   : Baseline consensus (fraction of variants per score class)
#   • Middle ring  : Delta ring — shows variants that are APR (activity reduced
#                    in presence of mercury) or Improved under metal exposure
#   • Inner ring   : Metal map consensus
#   • Lollipops    : Clinical reference variants (HCP/HP = purple, benign = green)
#   • Secondary structure: helices (sine waves), strands (rectangles + arrowheads),
#                    loops (lines) fetched live from UniProt
#
# Score class boundaries (LOF → GOF):
#   lof1 ≤ 0.0 < lof3 ≤ 0.2 < lof4 ≤ 0.4 < lof5 ≤ 0.6 < lof6 ≤ 0.8
#   wt   ≤ 1.2 < gof1 ≤ 1.4 < gof2 ≤ 1.6 < gof3 ≤ 1.8 < gof4 ≤ 2.0
#   gof6  > 2.0
# =============================================================================

DATA_PATH <- "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx"

UNIPROT_ID <- "P36551"

class_levels <- c(
  "lof1","lof3","lof4","lof5","lof6",
  "wt",
  "gof1","gof2","gof3","gof4","gof6"
)

color_map <- c(
  lof1 = rgb(0.23, 0.37, 0.80),
  lof3 = rgb(0.48, 0.58, 0.87),
  lof4 = rgb(0.61, 0.69, 0.90),
  lof5 = rgb(0.74, 0.79, 0.93),
  lof6 = rgb(0.87, 0.89, 0.96),
  wt   = rgb(1, 1, 1),
  gof1 = rgb(0.96, 0.85, 0.85),
  gof2 = rgb(0.93, 0.71, 0.71),
  gof3 = rgb(0.90, 0.57, 0.57),
  gof4 = rgb(0.87, 0.43, 0.43),
  gof6 = rgb(0.80, 0.15, 0.15)
)

delta_colors <- c(
  turquoise  = "#2ED1D1",
  darkorange = "darkorange",
  white      = "white"
)

# ── Radial geometry parameters ────────────────────────────────────────────
ring_width        <- 0.45
delta_ring_height <- 0.03
ring_gap          <- 0.01

metal_inner_radius    <- 0.40
delta_inner_radius    <- metal_inner_radius + ring_width + ring_gap
baseline_inner_radius <- delta_inner_radius + delta_ring_height + ring_gap
structure_radius      <- baseline_inner_radius + ring_width + 0.15

lollipop_distance    <- 0.01
lollipop_height      <- 0.12
lollipop_base_radius <- structure_radius + lollipop_distance

gap_start <- 454
gap_width <- 20
gap_pos   <- (gap_start + 1):(gap_start + gap_width)

# ── Fetch UniProt secondary structure annotation ──────────────────────────
get_uniprot_secstruc <- function(uniprot_id) {
  js <- fromJSON(content(
    GET(paste0("https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/", uniprot_id, ".json")),
    "text"
  ))
  
  sec <- tibble(
    pos      = 1:nchar(js$sequence$value),
    secstruc = "other"
  )
  
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(js$features))) {
    f <- js$features[i, ]
    if (f$type %in% c("Helix", "Beta strand")) {
      s <- as.integer(f$location$start$value)
      e <- as.integer(f$location$end$value)
      sec$secstruc[s:e] <- ifelse(f$type == "Helix", "helix", "strand")
    }
  }
  sec
}

sec_df <- get_uniprot_secstruc(UNIPROT_ID)

# ── Build consensus rings ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
build_consensus <- function(path, sheet, r_inner, r_height, flip = FALSE) {
  df <- read_excel(path, sheet = sheet, skip = 1) %>%
    mutate(pos = as.integer(str_extract(hgvs_pro, "\\d+"))) %>%
    filter(!is.na(pos), !is.na(score)) %>%
    mutate(class = case_when(
      score <= 0.0 ~ "lof1", score <= 0.2 ~ "lof3", score <= 0.4 ~ "lof4",
      score <= 0.6 ~ "lof5", score <= 0.8 ~ "lof6", score <= 1.2 ~ "wt",
      score <= 1.4 ~ "gof1", score <= 1.6 ~ "gof2", score <= 1.8 ~ "gof3",
      score <= 2.0 ~ "gof4", TRUE ~ "gof6"
    ))
  
  out <- df %>%
    count(pos, class, name = "n") %>%
    mutate(class = factor(class, levels = class_levels)) %>%
    arrange(pos, class) %>%
    group_by(pos) %>%
    mutate(
      frac = n / sum(n),
      ymin = if (!flip) r_inner + lag(cumsum(frac), default = 0) * r_height
      else r_inner + r_height - cumsum(frac) * r_height,
      ymax = if (!flip) r_inner + cumsum(frac) * r_height
      else r_inner + r_height - lag(cumsum(frac), default = 0) * r_height
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  bind_rows(
    out,
    tibble(
      pos   = gap_pos,
      class = factor(NA, levels = class_levels),
      ymin  = r_inner,
      ymax  = r_inner + r_height
    )
  )
}

# ── Build delta ring ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
build_delta_ring <- function(delta_path, delta_sheet, baseline_path, baseline_sheet, r_inner, r_height) {
  
  delta <- read_excel(delta_path, sheet = delta_sheet, skip = 1) %>%
    mutate(
      deltaScore  = as.numeric(deltaScore),
      deltaSE     = as.numeric(deltaSE),
      pos         = as.integer(str_extract(hgvs_pro, "\\d+")),
      significant = case_when(
      significant == "TRUE"  ~ TRUE,
      significant == "FALSE" ~ FALSE,
      TRUE                   ~ NA
)    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(pos), is.finite(deltaScore), is.finite(deltaSE), !is.na(significant))
  setDT(delta)
  
  baseline_map <- read_excel(baseline_path, sheet = baseline_sheet, skip = 1)
  setDT(baseline_map)
  colnames(baseline_map)[1:4] <- c("hgvs_pro", "baseline_score", "se", "df")
  
  baseline_map[, baseline_lower := baseline_score + se]
  baseline_stop <- grepl("Ter$", baseline_map$hgvs_pro)
  baseline_syn  <- grepl("=$",   baseline_map$hgvs_pro)
  baseline_miss <- !(baseline_stop | baseline_syn)
  
  baseline_miss_tolerated <- baseline_map[
    baseline_miss & baseline_lower >= 0.5
  ]
  
  delta[, Impact := "neutral"]
  APR_set      <- delta[significant == TRUE  & deltaScore < 0 & hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated$hgvs_pro, hgvs_pro]
  Improved_set <- delta[significant == TRUE  & deltaScore > 0 & hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated$hgvs_pro, hgvs_pro]
  known_set    <- delta[significant == FALSE & hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated$hgvs_pro, hgvs_pro]
  
  delta[hgvs_pro %in% APR_set,      Impact := "APR"]
  delta[hgvs_pro %in% Improved_set, Impact := "improved"]
  delta[hgvs_pro %in% known_set,    Impact := "known"]
  
  delta_pos <- delta %>%
    group_by(pos, Impact) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(pos) %>%
    mutate(
      max_n    = max(n),
      n_at_max = sum(n == max_n)
    ) %>%
    slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      delta_bin = case_when(
        Impact == "APR"      & n_at_max == 1 ~ "turquoise",
        Impact == "improved" & n_at_max == 1 ~ "darkorange",
        TRUE                                 ~ "white"
      ),
      ymin = r_inner,
      ymax = r_inner + r_height
    ) %>%
    select(pos, delta_bin, ymin, ymax)
  
  bind_rows(
    delta_pos,
    tibble(pos = gap_pos, delta_bin = "white", ymin = r_inner, ymax = r_inner + r_height)
  )
}

# ── Secondary structure elements ──────────────────────────────────────────
make_ss_elements <- function(sec_df, r) {
  sec_df <- bind_rows(sec_df, tibble(pos = gap_pos, secstruc = "gap"))
  helices <- strands <- arrowheads <- loops <- tibble()
  
  i <- 1
  while (i <= nrow(sec_df)) {
    t <- sec_df$secstruc[i]
    s <- sec_df$pos[i]
    while (i < nrow(sec_df) && sec_df$secstruc[i + 1] == t) i <- i + 1
    e <- sec_df$pos[i]
    
    if (t == "helix") {
      x <- seq(s, e, length.out = 80)
      y <- r + 0.08 * sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 80))
      helices <- bind_rows(helices, tibble(x, y, group = s))
    } else if (t == "strand") {
      h <- 0.12
      strands <- bind_rows(strands, tibble(
        x = c(s, e, e, s), y = c(r - h/2, r - h/2, r + h/2, r + h/2), group = s
      ))
      arrowheads <- bind_rows(arrowheads, tibble(
        x = c(e, e + 3, e), y = c(r - h/2, r, r + h/2), group = s
      ))
    } else {
      loops <- bind_rows(loops, tibble(x = c(s, e), y = c(r, r), group = s))
    }
    i <- i + 1
  }
  
  list(helices, strands, arrowheads, loops)
}

ss <- make_ss_elements(sec_df, structure_radius)

# ── Clinical reference lollipops ──────────────────────────────────────────
ref <- read_excel(DATA_PATH, sheet = "ReferenceSet", skip = 1) %>%
  mutate(
    pos = as.integer(str_extract(hgvs_pro, "\\d+")),
    col = case_when(
      disease_type %in% c("HCP", "HP") ~ "#AF22DD",
      disease_type == "VUS"            ~ "grey50",
      disease_type == "none"           ~ "#50DD22",
      TRUE                             ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(pos), !is.na(col))

# ── Build all rings ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
metal <- build_consensus(
  DATA_PATH, "Mercury Map",
  metal_inner_radius, ring_width, flip = TRUE
)

delta <- build_delta_ring(
  DATA_PATH, "Delta Map",
  DATA_PATH, "Baseline Map",
  delta_inner_radius, delta_ring_height
)

base <- build_consensus(
  DATA_PATH, "Baseline Map",
  baseline_inner_radius, ring_width
)

axis_breaks <- c(1, 100, 200, 300, 400)

# ── Shared plot builder ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
build_plot <- function() {
  ggplot() +
    geom_rect(
      data = metal,
      aes(xmin = pos - 0.5, xmax = pos + 0.5, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = class),
      color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.2
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_map, na.value = "white") +
    new_scale_fill() +
    geom_rect(
      data = delta,
      aes(xmin = pos - 0.5, xmax = pos + 0.5, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = delta_bin),
      color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.2
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = delta_colors, na.value = "white") +
    new_scale_fill() +
    geom_rect(
      data = base,
      aes(xmin = pos - 0.5, xmax = pos + 0.5, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = class),
      color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.2
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_map, na.value = "white") +
    geom_segment(
      data = ref,
      aes(x = pos, xend = pos,
          y = lollipop_base_radius,
          yend = lollipop_base_radius + lollipop_height),
      color = ref$col, linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = ref,
      aes(x = pos, y = lollipop_base_radius + lollipop_height),
      color = ref$col, size = 1
    ) +
    geom_path(data = ss[[4]], aes(x, y, group = group), color = "grey40", linewidth = 2)  +
    geom_polygon(data = ss[[2]], aes(x, y, group = group), fill = "black")                +
    geom_polygon(data = ss[[3]], aes(x, y, group = group), fill = "black")                +
    geom_line(data = ss[[1]], aes(x, y, group = group), color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    coord_polar(start = -pi/2) +
    ylim(0, lollipop_base_radius + lollipop_height + 0.1) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = axis_breaks, labels = axis_breaks) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x        = element_text(color = "black", size = 10),
      panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.2),
      axis.ticks         = element_line(color = "black")
    )
}

# ── Render interactive preview ────────────────────────────────────────────
build_plot()

# ── Export to PDF ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pdf(file = "~/Desktop/Fig1_Maps.pdf", width = 8, height = 8, useDingbats = FALSE)
print(build_plot())
dev.off()

# ── Count residue positions where the dominant delta impact is APR (teal) ──
baseline_map <- read_excel(DATA_PATH, sheet = "Baseline Map", skip = 1)
colnames(baseline_map)[1:4] <- c("hgvs_pro", "baseline_score", "se", "df")
baseline_map <- baseline_map %>% mutate(baseline_lower = baseline_score + se)

baseline_miss_tolerated <- baseline_map %>%
  filter(!grepl("Ter$", hgvs_pro), !grepl("=$", hgvs_pro), baseline_lower >= 0.5)

teal_positions <- delta$pos[delta$delta_bin == "turquoise"]

delta_raw <- read_excel(DATA_PATH, sheet = "Delta Map", skip = 1) %>%
  mutate(
    deltaScore  = as.numeric(deltaScore),
    deltaSE     = as.numeric(deltaSE),
    pos         = as.integer(str_extract(hgvs_pro, "\\d+")),
    significant = case_when(
    significant == "TRUE"  ~ TRUE,
    significant == "FALSE" ~ FALSE,
  TRUE                   ~ NA
)  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(significant))

delta_raw %>%
  filter(
    pos %in% teal_positions,
    significant == TRUE,
    deltaScore < 0,
    hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated$hgvs_pro
  ) %>%
  summarise(n_positions = n_distinct(pos), n_variants = n())
```


```{r, Population Level Assessment, eval=FALSE}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 3 — Population-Level Assessment
#
# Tests whether the DMS functional scores are depleted for deleterious variants
# in the human population, as expected under purifying selection.
#
# Method: log odds ratio comparing the observed frequency of deleterious /
# neutral DMS variants in population databases vs. their expected frequency
# under no selection. Fisher's exact test provides 95% CIs.
#
# Population sources:
#   • UK Biobank — allele frequencies from the UK Biobank exome release
#   • gnomAD v4  — population allele frequencies (gnomAD missense variants)
# =============================================================================

# ── Log odds ratio function ────────────────────────────────────────────────
calculate_log_odds_ci <- function(CPOX_data, human_pop_data,
                                  deleterious_threshold, neutral_threshold) {
  
  deleterious_variants <- CPOX_data %>%
    filter(score < deleterious_threshold) %>%
    select(hgvs_pro)
  
  neutral_variants <- CPOX_data %>%
    filter(score > neutral_threshold) %>%
    select(hgvs_pro)
  
  # Observed: how many DMS variants appear in the population dataset
  observed_del <- sum(deleterious_variants$hgvs_pro %in% human_pop_data$hgvs_pro)
  total_del    <- nrow(deleterious_variants)
  expected_del <- (nrow(human_pop_data) / nrow(CPOX_data)) * total_del
  
  observed_neu <- sum(neutral_variants$hgvs_pro %in% human_pop_data$hgvs_pro)
  total_neu    <- nrow(neutral_variants)
  expected_neu <- (nrow(human_pop_data) / nrow(CPOX_data)) * total_neu
  
  # 2×2 contingency tables: observed vs. expected
  del_table <- matrix(
    c(observed_del, total_del - observed_del,
      expected_del, total_del - expected_del),
    nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE
  )
  
  neu_table <- matrix(
    c(observed_neu, total_neu - observed_neu,
      expected_neu, total_neu - expected_neu),
    nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE
  )
  
  log_odds_del <- log((del_table[1,1] * del_table[2,2]) / (del_table[1,2] * del_table[2,1]))
  log_odds_neu <- log((neu_table[1,1] * neu_table[2,2]) / (neu_table[1,2] * neu_table[2,1]))
  
  fisher_del <- fisher.test(del_table)
  fisher_neu <- fisher.test(neu_table)
  
  list(
    log_odds_del = log_odds_del,
    log_odds_neu = log_odds_neu,
    ci_del       = log(fisher_del$conf.int),
    ci_neu       = log(fisher_neu$conf.int)
  )
}

# ── UK Biobank ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# NOTE: UKBiobank sheet must be present in Manuscript_Data.xlsx
UKBiobank <- tryCatch({
  df <- read_sheet("UKBiobank")
  if (nrow(df) == 0) stop("empty")
  # Build HGVS protein notation from single-letter amino acid columns
  df$hgvs_pro <- paste0(
    substr(df$Amino_acids, 1, 1),
    df$Protein_position,
    substr(df$Amino_acids, nchar(df$Amino_acids), nchar(df$Amino_acids))
  )
  # Convert single-letter amino acid codes to three-letter (e.g. "A123V" → "Ala123Val")
  df$hgvs_pro <- mgsub(
    df$hgvs_pro,
    pattern     = c("A","R","N","D","C","Q","E","G","H","I","L","K","M","F","P","O","S","U","T","W","Y","V"),
    replacement = c("Ala","Arg","Asn","Asp","Cys","Gln","Glu","Gly","His","Ile","Leu","Lys","Met","Phe","Pro","Pyl","Ser","Sec","Thr","Trp","Tyr","Val")
  )
  df$hgvs_pro <- paste0("p.", df$hgvs_pro)
  df <- df %>%
    distinct(hgvs_pro, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    filter(!grepl("X|\\*", hgvs_pro)) %>%
    select(hgvs_pro, UKB_AF)
  colnames(df)[2] <- "frequency"
  df
}, error = function(e) {
  warning("UKBiobank sheet missing or empty in Manuscript_Data.xlsx — skipping. ",
          "Add UKBiobank.csv data to that sheet (info row in row 1, headers in row 2).")
  data.frame(hgvs_pro = character(), frequency = numeric())
})

# ── gnomAD v4 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# NOTE: gnomADv4_Data sheet must be present in Manuscript_Data.xlsx
gnomad <- tryCatch({
  df <- read_sheet("gnomADv4_Data")
  if (nrow(df) == 0) stop("empty")
  colnames(df)[12] <- "hgvs_pro"
  colnames(df)[14] <- "type"
  colnames(df)[20] <- "frequency"
  df %>%
    filter(type == "missense_variant") %>%
    select(hgvs_pro, frequency) %>%
    distinct(hgvs_pro, .keep_all = TRUE)
}, error = function(e) {
  warning("gnomADv4_Data sheet missing or empty in Manuscript_Data.xlsx — skipping. ",
          "Add gnomADv4_Data.csv data to that sheet (info row in row 1, headers in row 2).")
  data.frame(hgvs_pro = character(), frequency = numeric())
})

# ── Combine population datasets ────────────────────────────────────────────
HumanPop_Frequency <- bind_rows(UKBiobank, gnomad) %>%
  distinct(hgvs_pro, .keep_all = TRUE)

cat("Total unique variants in combined population dataset:", nrow(HumanPop_Frequency), "\n")

# ── Calculate log odds ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
baseline <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")

deleterious_threshold <- 0.5
neutral_threshold     <- 0.5

log_odds <- calculate_log_odds_ci(baseline, HumanPop_Frequency,
                                   deleterious_threshold, neutral_threshold)

log_odds_data <- data.frame(
  category = rep("baseline", 2),
  Type     = c("deleterious", "neutral"),
  Log_Odds = c(log_odds$log_odds_del, log_odds$log_odds_neu),
  CI_Lower = c(log_odds$ci_del[1],   log_odds$ci_neu[1]),
  CI_Upper = c(log_odds$ci_del[2],   log_odds$ci_neu[2])
)

# Point-range plot (log odds with 95% CI)
pointRangeOdds <- ggplot(log_odds_data, aes(x = Type, y = Log_Odds)) +
  geom_pointrange(
    aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper, color = category),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    size = 0.5
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("baseline" = "black")) +
  labs(x = "Variant Type", y = "Log Odds Ratio") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  coord_flip()

print(pointRangeOdds)
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Comparison of Baseline and Metal Map Scores}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 4 — Baseline vs. Metal Map Correlation
#
# Tests the global agreement between the baseline and metal-condition DMS maps.
# High correlation indicates that relative variant effects are preserved under
# metal exposure; divergence reveals condition-specific responses.
#
# Pearson r is used (linear relationship expected between scaled scores).
# Dashed orange lines at score = 0.5 mark the LOF/functional boundary.
# =============================================================================

baseline_map <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
metal_map    <- read_sheet("Mercury Map")

names(baseline_map)[names(baseline_map) == "score"] <- "baseline"
names(metal_map)[names(metal_map)       == "score"] <- "metal"

merged_maps     <- merge(baseline_map, metal_map, by = "hgvs_pro")
merged_maps$pos <- as.numeric(gsub("\\D", "", merged_maps$hgvs_pro))

cor_pear <- cor.test(merged_maps$baseline, merged_maps$metal, method = "pearson")
cat(sprintf("Pearson r = %.2f, p = %.3g\n", cor_pear$estimate, cor_pear$p.value))
r_value_spear <- cor(merged_maps$baseline, merged_maps$metal, method = "spearman")

maps_corr <- ggplot(merged_maps, aes(x = baseline, y = metal)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "turquoise", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkorange") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkorange") +
  labs(x = "Baseline CPOX Map", y = "Conditional CPOX Map \n (Metal;Hg)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = max(merged_maps$baseline),
    y = min(merged_maps$metal),
    label = paste("R = ", round(cor_pear$estimate, 2)),
    hjust = 1
  )

pdf("~/Desktop/BetweenMapCorrelation.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
print(maps_corr)
dev.off()
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Examining Delta Scores}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 5 — Delta Score Analysis
#
# The delta score captures the change in functional fitness between the
# baseline and metal (Hg) conditions: delta = baseline_logPhi – metal_logPhi
# Positive delta → variant tolerates baseline but not metal (APR)
# Negative delta → variant is improved by metal exposure
#
# This chunk:
#   1. Tests whether there is a systematic shift using a paired Wilcoxon test
#   2. Plots a mirrored bar histogram: controls (synonymous + nonsense) above
#      zero, missense below zero
#   3. Plots the full distribution of all delta scores
# =============================================================================

delta_map <- read_sheet("Delta Map") %>% na.omit()

# Overall median shift between conditions
median_diff <- median(
  delta_map$logPhi_weighted_mean_metal - delta_map$logPhi_weighted_mean_baseline,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

# Paired Wilcoxon: tests whether the two conditions differ per variant
wilcox_res <- wilcox.test(
  delta_map$logPhi_weighted_mean_baseline,
  delta_map$logPhi_weighted_mean_metal,
  paired      = TRUE,
  alternative = "two.sided",
  exact       = FALSE
)

cat("Median difference (metal - baseline):", median_diff, "\n")
print(wilcox_res)

process_delta <- function(delta_path) {
  
  data <- read_sheet("Delta Map") %>% na.omit()
  
  stop_idx  <- grepl("Ter$", data$hgvs_pro)
  syn_idx   <- grepl("=$",   data$hgvs_pro)
  miss_idx  <- !(stop_idx | syn_idx)
  
  cntl_scores <- data$deltaScore[stop_idx | syn_idx]
  miss_scores <- data$deltaScore[miss_idx]
  
  # ── Plot 1: Mirrored density histogram ────────────────────────────────
  hist_breaks <- seq(-3, 3, by = 0.1)
  cntl_hist   <- hist(cntl_scores, breaks = hist_breaks, plot = FALSE)
  miss_hist   <- hist(miss_scores, breaks = hist_breaks, plot = FALSE)
  
  plot_df <- rbind(
    data.frame(bin = cntl_hist$mids, density = cntl_hist$density,   type = "cntl"),
    data.frame(bin = miss_hist$mids, density = -miss_hist$density,  type = "missense")
  )
  
  Grouped_Delta <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = bin, y = density, fill = type)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.095) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("cntl" = "black", "missense" = "grey60")) +
    labs(
      title = "Delta Score Density: Controls vs Missense",
      x = "Delta Score",
      y = "Density"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 14), axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.position = "top")
  
  pdf("~/Desktop/group_Delta.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
  print(Grouped_Delta)
  dev.off()
  
  # ── Plot 2: Full delta score distribution ──────────────────────────────
  complete_Delta_dis <- ggplot(
    data.frame(deltaScore = data$deltaScore),
    aes(x = deltaScore)
  ) +
    geom_histogram(
      aes(y = after_stat(density)),
      binwidth = 0.05, fill = "grey40", color = "black", alpha = 0.6
    ) +
    geom_density(linewidth = 1.2) +
    labs(title = "Distribution of All Delta Scores", x = "Delta Score", y = "Density") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 14), axis.text = element_text(size = 12))
  
  pdf("~/Desktop/complete_Delta.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
  print(complete_Delta_dis)
  dev.off()
}

process_delta("~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx")  # reads sheet "Delta Map"
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Comparison of Baseline & Metal Map Scores with Predicted ΔΔG Scores}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 6 — Functional Scores vs. Predicted ΔΔG
#
# Compares DMS scores (baseline and metal) to in silico thermodynamic
# stability predictions (ΔΔG) based on the CPOX crystal structure (PDB: 2AEX).
#
# Workflow:
#   1. Convert ΔΔG variant notation to HGVS
#   2. Merge with DMS scores
#   3. Compute 15-aa rolling means per position
#   4. Plot rolling mean traces for baseline, metal, and ΔΔG
#   5. Pearson correlation with BH-adjusted p-values
#   6. Scatter plots with r annotation
# =============================================================================

ddG_scores <- read_sheet("ddG Values")
#ddG_scores <- subset(ddG_scores, CHAIN != "")

# Single-letter → three-letter amino acid dictionary
aa_dict <- c("A"="Ala","R"="Arg","N"="Asn","D"="Asp","C"="Cys","Q"="Gln",
             "E"="Glu","G"="Gly","H"="His","I"="Ile","L"="Leu","K"="Lys",
             "M"="Met","F"="Phe","P"="Pro","S"="Ser","T"="Thr","W"="Trp",
             "Y"="Tyr","V"="Val")



# Convert "A123V" → "p.Ala123Val"
replace_aa <- function(s) {
  for (aa in names(aa_dict)) s <- gsub(aa, aa_dict[aa], s)
  return(paste0("p.", s))
}

ddG_scores$VARIANT <- replace_aa(ddG_scores$VARIANT)
ddG_scores <- ddG_scores[, c(1, 3)]
colnames(ddG_scores) <- c("hgvs_pro", "ddG")

ddG_scores$hgvs_pro <- ddG_scores$hgvs_pro %>%
  gsub("Glyln", "Gln", .) %>%
  gsub("Glylu", "Glu", .) %>%
  gsub("Prohe", "Phe", .)

# Load and rename map columns
baseline_map <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
colnames(baseline_map)[2:4] <- c("baseline", "baselineSE", "baselineDF")

metal_map <- read_sheet("Mercury Map")
setDT(metal_map)
colnames(metal_map)[2:4] <- c("metal", "metalSE", "metalDF")

# Merge and remove synonymous variants (regex: same AA before and after position)
Stability <- ddG_scores |>
  merge(baseline_map, by = "hgvs_pro") |>
  merge(metal_map,    by = "hgvs_pro") |>
  subset(!grepl("(\\w\\d+)(\\1)", hgvs_pro)) |>
  unique()

Stability$pos <- as.numeric(gsub("\\D", "", Stability$hgvs_pro))

# 15-aa rolling mean smoothing for positional trends
Rolling_Stability <- Stability |>
  dplyr::group_by(pos) |>
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_baseline = mean(baseline,       na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_metal    = mean(metal,          na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_ddG      = mean(as.integer(ddG), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups       = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    rolling_baseline = zoo::rollmean(mean_baseline, k = 15, fill = NA, align = "center"),
    rolling_metal    = zoo::rollmean(mean_metal,    k = 15, fill = NA, align = "center"),
    rolling_ddG      = zoo::rollmean(mean_ddG,      k = 15, fill = NA, align = "center")
  )

# Melt to long format for faceted plotting
Rolling_Stability_long <- reshape2::melt(
  Rolling_Stability[, c("pos","rolling_baseline","rolling_metal","rolling_ddG")],
  id.vars       = "pos",
  variable.name = "Metric",
  value.name    = "Score"
)

# Rolling mean line plot per metric
plot_metric <- function(data, metric_name, color, ylab) {
  ggplot(data[data$Metric == metric_name, ], aes(x = pos, y = Score)) +
    geom_line(size = 0.5, color = color) +
    labs(x = "Amino Acid Position", y = ylab) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = seq(0, 450, by = 50), color = "grey", linetype = "dotted") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey", linetype = "dotted"),
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1)) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = range(data$pos, na.rm = TRUE))
}

p1 <- plot_metric(Rolling_Stability_long, "rolling_baseline", "#11EE89", "Baseline Score\n(Moving Avg, 15 AA)")
p2 <- plot_metric(Rolling_Stability_long, "rolling_metal",    "#EE1176", "Metal Score\n(Moving Avg, 15 AA)")
p3 <- plot_metric(Rolling_Stability_long, "rolling_ddG",      "black",   "Predicted ΔΔG\n(Moving Avg, 15 AA)")
gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, ncol = 1)

# Pearson correlations between functional scores and ΔΔG
cor_baseline <- cor.test(Stability$baseline, Stability$ddG, method = "pearson")
cor_metal    <- cor.test(Stability$metal,    Stability$ddG, method = "pearson")

# BH-adjusted p-values (two tests)
raw_pvals <- c(cor_baseline$p.value, cor_metal$p.value)
padj      <- p.adjust(raw_pvals, method = "BH")
names(padj) <- c("Baseline vs ΔΔG", "Metal vs ΔΔG")

cat(sprintf("Baseline vs ΔΔG: Pearson r = %.2f, p = %.3g, padj = %.3g\n",
            cor_baseline$estimate, cor_baseline$p.value, padj[1]))
cat(sprintf("Metal vs ΔΔG:    Pearson r = %.2f, p = %.3g, padj = %.3g\n",
            cor_metal$estimate, cor_metal$p.value, padj[2]))

# Scatter plot helper
plot_scatter <- function(x, y, cor_result, padj_value, title, color) {
  ggplot(Stability, aes_string(x = x, y = y)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = color) +
    annotate("text",
             x     = max(Stability[[x]], na.rm = TRUE),
             y     = min(Stability[[y]], na.rm = TRUE),
             label = sprintf("r = %.2f\np = %.3g", cor_result$estimate, cor_result$p.value),
             hjust = 1, vjust = 0, size = 4) +
    labs(x = x, y = "Predicted ΔΔG Score", title = title) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey", linetype = "dotted"),
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0),
          axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0))
}

scatter1 <- plot_scatter("baseline", "ddG", cor_baseline, padj[1], "Baseline Score vs. ΔΔG", "turquoise")
scatter2 <- plot_scatter("metal",    "ddG", cor_metal,    padj[2], "Metal Score vs. ΔΔG",    "purple")

pdf("~/Desktop/ddG&MapCorrelation.pdf",  width = 8, height = 6); print(scatter1); dev.off()
pdf("~/Desktop/ddG&MapCorrelation2.pdf", width = 8, height = 6); print(scatter2); dev.off()
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Comparison of Baseline & Metal Map Scores with Patient Enzymatic Activity}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 7 — Correlation with Patient Enzymatic Activity
#
# Tests whether the DMS functional score predicts residual CPOX enzymatic
# activity measured in HCP patients. Activity is expressed as a percentage
# of the lower reference value.
#
# Both Pearson and Spearman correlations are reported. Median activity per
# variant is used (multiple patients may carry the same variant).
# =============================================================================

process_activity_correlation <- function(baseline_path = NULL,
                                          metal_path    = NULL,
                                          activity_path = NULL) {
  
  # Inner helper: merge map scores with activity data and aggregate per variant
  prepare_correlation_data <- function(map_data, activity_data, score_column) {
    merged <- merge(activity_data, map_data, by = "hgvs_pro")
    # Filter to rows where HCP patient enzyme activity is present
    merged <- merged[!is.na(merged$hcp_enzyme_activity), ]
    merged <- merged[, c("hgvs_pro", "pct_activity", score_column)]
    
    # Median activity per variant (collapses multiple patient entries)
    median_activity <- aggregate(
      pct_activity ~ hgvs_pro,
      data = merged, FUN = median, na.rm = TRUE
    )
    colnames(median_activity) <- c("hgvs_pro", "median_activity")
    
    out <- merge(map_data, median_activity, by = "hgvs_pro")
    out[complete.cases(out), ]
  }
  
  if (!is.null(activity_path)) {
    activity_data <- read_sheet("Enzymatic Activity")
    if (nrow(activity_data) == 0 || ncol(activity_data) == 0) {
      warning("Enzymatic Activity sheet is empty in Manuscript_Data.xlsx. ",
              "Please paste the CPOX_activities.csv data into that sheet ",
              "(with a generic info row in row 1 and column headers in row 2). ",
              "Skipping activity correlation.")
      return(invisible(NULL))
    }
    # Rename by position: col names contain newlines and special characters
    # Col 4 = Amino acid change (hgvs_pro)
    # Col 5 = HCP patients Enzyme activity
    # Col 8 = % of activity compared to the lower reference value
    colnames(activity_data)[c(4, 5, 8)] <- c("hgvs_pro", "hcp_enzyme_activity", "pct_activity")
    activity_data$hgvs_pro            <- as.character(activity_data$hgvs_pro)
    activity_data$hcp_enzyme_activity <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(activity_data$hcp_enzyme_activity))
    activity_data$pct_activity        <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(activity_data$pct_activity))
  } else {
    stop("Activity data required.")
  }
  
  # ── BASELINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if (!is.null(baseline_path)) {
    baseline_map <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
    colnames(baseline_map)[2] <- "baseline_score"
    baseline_map <- baseline_map[, c("hgvs_pro", "baseline_score")]
    
    baseline_data <- prepare_correlation_data(baseline_map, activity_data, "baseline_score")
    
    spearman_baseline <- cor.test(baseline_data$median_activity, baseline_data$baseline_score, method = "spearman")
    pearson_baseline  <- cor.test(baseline_data$median_activity, baseline_data$baseline_score, method = "pearson")
    
    print(paste("Spearman (Baseline):", round(spearman_baseline$estimate, 2),
                "P =", signif(spearman_baseline$p.value, 3)))
    print(paste("Pearson (Baseline):",  round(pearson_baseline$estimate,  2),
                "P =", signif(pearson_baseline$p.value,  3)))
    
    pdf("~/Desktop/Baseline_Activity.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
    plot(baseline_data$median_activity, baseline_data$baseline_score,
         xlab = "Activity (% of lower reference value)",
         ylab = "Baseline Score",
         main = paste("Spearman r =", round(spearman_baseline$estimate, 2),
                      "p =", signif(spearman_baseline$p.value, 2),
                      "\nPearson r =", round(pearson_baseline$estimate, 2),
                      "p =", signif(pearson_baseline$p.value, 2)),
         pch = 19, col = "grey40")
    abline(lm(baseline_score ~ median_activity, data = baseline_data), lwd = 2)
    dev.off()
  }
  
  # ── METAL ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if (!is.null(metal_path)) {
    metal_map <- read_sheet("Mercury Map")
    colnames(metal_map)[2] <- "metal_score"
    metal_map <- metal_map[, c("hgvs_pro", "metal_score")]
    
    metal_data <- prepare_correlation_data(metal_map, activity_data, "metal_score")
    
    spearman_metal <- cor.test(metal_data$median_activity, metal_data$metal_score, method = "spearman")
    pearson_metal  <- cor.test(metal_data$median_activity, metal_data$metal_score, method = "pearson")
    
    print(paste("Spearman (Metal):", round(spearman_metal$estimate, 2),
                "P =", signif(spearman_metal$p.value, 3)))
    print(paste("Pearson (Metal):",  round(pearson_metal$estimate,  2),
                "P =", signif(pearson_metal$p.value,  3)))
    
    pdf("~/Desktop/Metal_Activity.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
    plot(metal_data$median_activity, metal_data$metal_score,
         xlab = "Activity (% of lower reference value)",
         ylab = "Metal Score",
         main = paste("Spearman r =", round(spearman_metal$estimate, 2),
                      "p =", signif(spearman_metal$p.value, 2),
                      "\nPearson r =", round(pearson_metal$estimate, 2),
                      "p =", signif(pearson_metal$p.value, 2)),
         pch = 19, col = "grey40")
    abline(lm(metal_score ~ median_activity, data = metal_data), lwd = 2)
    dev.off()
  }
}

process_activity_correlation(
  baseline_path = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",
  metal_path    = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",
  activity_path = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx"
)
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Hypercomplementing variant scores: Baseline Map vs AlphaMissense}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 8 — Hypercomplementing Variants vs. AlphaMissense
#
# Hypercomplementing variants are defined as those with baseline score > 1.2
# (above the WT reference range). These may represent gain-of-function or
# hyperstable variants.
#
# This chunk compares the DMS baseline score against AlphaMissense predictions
# for this subset. AlphaMissense scores are rescaled to [0,1] for direct
# comparison. Expected: AlphaMissense should score these variants as
# damaging (high score) while DMS shows elevated function.
# =============================================================================

baseline_file <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
setDT(baseline_file)
colnames(baseline_file)[2] <- "baseline_map"
baseline_file <- baseline_file[, .(hgvs_pro, baseline_map)]

AM_Data <- read_sheet("AlphaMissense Values")
setDT(AM_Data)
colnames(AM_Data)[1:2] <- c("hgvs_pro", "AM")

# Keep only hypercomplementing variants (score > 1.2)
Hyper_Data <- merge(AM_Data, baseline_file, by = "hgvs_pro")
Hyper_Data <- Hyper_Data[baseline_map > 1.2]
Hyper_Data <- na.omit(Hyper_Data)

# Rescale AlphaMissense to [0,1] for comparable y-axis with baseline
rescaled_AM  <- (Hyper_Data$AM - min(Hyper_Data$AM)) / (max(Hyper_Data$AM) - min(Hyper_Data$AM))
rescaled_Map <- Hyper_Data$baseline_map

Map_Preditor_Hyper_Scores <- data.table(
  AlphaMissense = rescaled_AM,
  BaselineMap   = rescaled_Map
)

Map_Preditor_Hyper_Scores_long <- melt(Map_Preditor_Hyper_Scores,
                                        variable.name = "Predictor",
                                        value.name    = "Score")

hypercomplementing <- ggplot(Map_Preditor_Hyper_Scores_long, aes(x = Predictor, y = Score)) +
  theme_classic() +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(color = alpha("black", 0.5), stroke = 0.5, width = 0.2) +
  ylab("Level of Functionality") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

print(hypercomplementing)

ggsave(filename = "~/Desktop/hypercomplementing.pdf",
       plot = hypercomplementing, device = "pdf", width = 8, height = 8)
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Dunham vs Baseline scores}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 9 — Dunham 33-Map Substitution Trend Comparison
#
# Tests whether the amino acid substitution trends in the CPOX baseline and
# metal maps are consistent with trends across 33 published DMS maps
# (Dunham & Beltrao, 2021).
#
# Focuses on the TO residue: for each destination amino acid, compares the
# median score across the CPOX maps and the Dunham aggregated matrix.
# Also compares baseline vs. metal directly.
# Spearman rank correlation is used throughout.
# =============================================================================

# ── Helper: pre-process missense variants ────────────────────────────────
process_map <- function(map) {
  map %>%
    filter(!str_detect(hgvs_pro, "=|Ter")) %>%
    mutate(
      from     = substr(hgvs_pro, 3, 5),
      pos      = str_extract(hgvs_pro, "\\d+"),
      changeTo = substr(hgvs_pro, nchar(hgvs_pro) - 2, nchar(hgvs_pro)),
      aa_type_From = case_when(
        from %in% c("Ala","Gly","Val","Leu","Ile","Met","Phe","Trp","Tyr") ~ "hydrophobic",
        from %in% c("Ser","Thr","Asn","Gln","Cys")                        ~ "polar",
        from %in% c("Asp","Glu","Arg","Lys","His")                        ~ "charged",
        from %in% c("Pro")                                                  ~ "proline",
        TRUE                                                                ~ "unknown"
      ),
      aa_type_To = case_when(
        changeTo %in% c("Ala","Gly","Val","Leu","Ile","Met","Phe","Trp","Tyr") ~ "hydrophobic",
        changeTo %in% c("Ser","Thr","Asn","Gln","Cys")                        ~ "polar",
        changeTo %in% c("Asp","Glu")                                           ~ "neg_charged",
        changeTo %in% c("Arg","Lys","His")                                     ~ "pos_charged",
        changeTo %in% c("Pro")                                                  ~ "proline",
        TRUE                                                                     ~ "unknown"
      )
    )
}

baseline_map       <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
baseline_processed <- process_map(baseline_map)

# Median score per destination amino acid in baseline map
median_scores_baseline_to <- baseline_processed %>%
  group_by(changeTo) %>%
  summarise(median_score_baseline_to = median(score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Read Dunham matrix and set synonymous positions to NA
Dunham                 <- read_sheet("Dunham Maps")
AminoAcidChangesDunham <- Dunham[, 4:24]

AminoAcidChangesDunham <- AminoAcidChangesDunham %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(cur_column() == wt, NA, .))) %>%
  ungroup()

# Median per destination AA in Dunham matrix
median_Dunham <- AminoAcidChangesDunham %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ median(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "changeTo", values_to = "median_dunham_to")

# Convert single-letter AA codes to three-letter
aa_replacements <- setNames(
  c("Ala","Arg","Asn","Asp","Cys","Gln","Glu","Gly","His","Ile","Leu","Lys",
    "Met","Phe","Pro","Pyl","Ser","Thr","Trp","Tyr","Val"),
  c("A","R","N","D","C","Q","E","G","H","I","L","K","M","F","P","O","S","T","W","Y","V")
)
median_Dunham$changeTo <- str_replace_all(median_Dunham$changeTo, aa_replacements)
# Fix multi-substitution artefacts from sequential string replacement
median_Dunham$changeTo <- median_Dunham$changeTo %>%
  gsub("Glyln", "Gln", .) %>%
  gsub("Glylu", "Glu", .) %>%
  gsub("Prohe", "Phe", .)

aggregate_dunham_maps_toAAscores <- merge(median_Dunham, median_scores_baseline_to, by = "changeTo")

# Scatter plot with Spearman correlation annotation
compare_variables <- function(data, var1, var2, label_var, x_label, y_label) {
  cor_test <- cor.test(data[[var1]], data[[var2]], method = "spearman")
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[var1]], y = .data[[var2]])) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(x = x_label, y = y_label) +
    ggtitle(paste("Correlation:", round(cor_test$estimate, 3),
                  "P-value:", format.pval(cor_test$p.value, digits = 3, format = "e"))) +
    geom_text_repel(aes(label = changeTo), size = 4, box.padding = 0.5)
  list(correlation = cor_test$estimate, p_value = cor_test$p.value)
  print(p)
}

result_baseline_Dunham <- compare_variables(
  aggregate_dunham_maps_toAAscores, "median_score_baseline_to", "median_dunham_to", "changeTo",
  "median score\n baseline map", "median score\n Dunham and Beltrao, 2021"
)

# ── Metal map comparisons ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
metal_map       <- read_sheet("Mercury Map")
metal_processed <- process_map(metal_map)

median_scores_metal_to <- metal_processed %>%
  group_by(changeTo) %>%
  summarise(median_score_metal_to = median(score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

aggregate_metal_maps_toAAscores <- merge(median_scores_baseline_to, median_scores_metal_to, by = "changeTo")
result_metal_baseline <- compare_variables(
  aggregate_metal_maps_toAAscores, "median_score_baseline_to", "median_score_metal_to", "changeTo",
  "median score\n baseline map", "median score\n metal map"
)

aggregate_dunham_metalmaps_toAAscores <- merge(median_Dunham, median_scores_metal_to, by = "changeTo")
result_metal_Dunham <- compare_variables(
  aggregate_dunham_metalmaps_toAAscores, "median_score_metal_to", "median_dunham_to", "changeTo",
  "median score\n metal map", "median score\n Dunham and Beltrao, 2021"
)
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Impact Based on Substitution/AA Type}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 10 — Amino Acid Substitution Type Analysis (FROM / TO)
#
# Tests whether the physicochemical class of the source (FROM) or destination
# (TO) amino acid predicts variant functional impact.
#
# FROM analysis: excludes self-substitutions; uses broad categories
#   (hydrophobic / polar / charged / proline)
# TO analysis:   uses detailed categories (splits charged into +/- for TO)
#   and performs binary comparisons (e.g. "TO proline" vs. "not proline")
#
# Statistics: pairwise Wilcoxon with Bonferroni correction.
# Plots saved automatically to ~/Desktop/.
# =============================================================================

library(data.table); library(dplyr); library(stringr); library(ggplot2); library(ggpubr)

baseline <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
metal    <- read_sheet("Mercury Map")

# Amino acid class definitions
aa_cats_basic <- list(
  hydrophobic = c("Ala","Gly","Val","Leu","Ile","Met","Phe","Trp","Tyr"),
  polar       = c("Ser","Thr","Asn","Gln","Cys"),
  charged     = c("Asp","Glu","Arg","Lys","His"),
  proline     = c("Pro")
)

# Detailed version: charged split into negative / positive for TO analysis
aa_cats_detailed <- list(
  hydrophobic = aa_cats_basic$hydrophobic,
  polar       = aa_cats_basic$polar,
  neg_charged = c("Asp","Glu"),
  pos_charged = c("Arg","Lys","His"),
  proline     = aa_cats_basic$proline
)

# Extract FROM/TO residues and assign amino acid class labels
process_map <- function(map, detailed = FALSE) {
  map_missense           <- map[!(grepl("=", map$hgvs_pro) | grepl("Ter", map$hgvs_pro)), ]
  map_missense$from      <- substr(map_missense$hgvs_pro, 3, 5)
  map_missense$pos       <- str_extract(map_missense$hgvs_pro, "[0-9]+")
  map_missense$changeTo  <- substr(map_missense$hgvs_pro,
                                    nchar(map_missense$hgvs_pro) - 2,
                                    nchar(map_missense$hgvs_pro))
  cats <- if (detailed) aa_cats_detailed else aa_cats_basic
  map_missense <- map_missense %>%
    mutate(
      aa_type_From = case_when(
        from %in% cats$hydrophobic ~ "hydrophobic",
        from %in% cats$polar ~ "polar",
        detailed & from %in% cats$neg_charged ~ "neg_charged",
        detailed & from %in% cats$pos_charged ~ "pos_charged",
        !detailed & from %in% cats$charged ~ "charged",
        from %in% cats$proline ~ "proline",
        TRUE ~ "unknown"
      ),
      aa_type_To = case_when(
        changeTo %in% cats$hydrophobic ~ "hydrophobic",
        changeTo %in% cats$polar ~ "polar",
        detailed & changeTo %in% cats$neg_charged ~ "neg_charged",
        detailed & changeTo %in% cats$pos_charged ~ "pos_charged",
        !detailed & changeTo %in% cats$charged ~ "charged",
        changeTo %in% cats$proline ~ "proline",
        TRUE ~ "unknown"
      )
    )
  return(map_missense)
}

# Unified analysis: pairwise Wilcoxon + boxplot with significance brackets
analyze_aa_category <- function(data, direction = c("FROM","TO"),
                                 simple_group = NULL, plot_title,
                                 pdf_filename, width = 6, height = 6) {
  direction <- match.arg(direction)
  aa_col    <- ifelse(direction == "FROM", "aa_type_From", "aa_type_To")
  
  if (direction == "FROM") data <- data[data$aa_type_From != data$aa_type_To, ]
  
  if (!is.null(simple_group)) {
    group_col <- paste0(aa_col, "_simple")
    data <- data %>%
      mutate(!!group_col := ifelse(get(aa_col) == simple_group, simple_group,
                                    paste0("not_", simple_group)))
    data[[group_col]] <- factor(data[[group_col]],
                                levels = c(simple_group, paste0("not_", simple_group)))
  } else {
    group_col         <- aa_col
    data[[group_col]] <- factor(data[[group_col]])
  }
  
  categories <- levels(data[[group_col]])
  if (length(categories) < 2) return(NULL)
  
  results <- data.frame()
  for (i in 1:(length(categories) - 1)) {
    for (j in (i + 1):length(categories)) {
      cat1 <- categories[i]; cat2 <- categories[j]
      g1   <- data$score[data[[group_col]] == cat1]
      g2   <- data$score[data[[group_col]] == cat2]
      test <- wilcox.test(g1, g2, correct = TRUE)
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        Category1 = cat1, Category2 = cat2,
        p.value   = test$p.value,
        Median1   = median(g1, na.rm = TRUE),
        Median2   = median(g2, na.rm = TRUE)
      ))
    }
  }
  results$p.adj <- p.adjust(results$p.value, method = "bonferroni")
  
  p <- ggplot(data, aes_string(x = group_col, y = "score")) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3) +
    labs(title = plot_title, x = paste(direction, "amino acid category"),
         y = "Variant functional score") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 17)
  
  sig_results <- results[results$p.adj < 0.05, ]
  if (nrow(sig_results) > 0) {
    comps <- mapply(c, sig_results$Category1, sig_results$Category2, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)
    p <- p + stat_compare_means(comparisons = comps, method = "wilcox.test",
                                label = "p.signif", p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
  }
  
  ggsave(paste0("~/Desktop/", pdf_filename), plot = p, device = "pdf",
         width = width, height = height)
  print(p)
}

baseline_basic    <- process_map(baseline, FALSE)
metal_basic       <- process_map(metal,    FALSE)
baseline_detailed <- process_map(baseline, TRUE)
metal_detailed    <- process_map(metal,    TRUE)

# FROM analyses (broad categories)
analyze_aa_category(baseline_basic, "FROM", NULL, "Baseline map: FROM amino acid type", "Baseline_FROM_full.pdf")
analyze_aa_category(metal_basic,    "FROM", NULL, "Metal map: FROM amino acid type",    "Metal_FROM_full.pdf")

# TO analyses — baseline (binary comparisons per class)
analyze_aa_category(baseline_detailed, "TO", "polar",       "Baseline map: TO polar vs others",           "Baseline_TO_polar.pdf")
analyze_aa_category(baseline_detailed, "TO", "hydrophobic", "Baseline map: TO hydrophobic vs others",     "Baseline_TO_hydrophobic.pdf")
analyze_aa_category(baseline_detailed, "TO", "proline",     "Baseline map: TO proline vs others",         "Baseline_TO_proline.pdf")
analyze_aa_category(baseline_detailed, "TO", "neg_charged", "Baseline map: TO negative charged vs others","Baseline_TO_neg_charged.pdf")

# TO analyses — metal map
analyze_aa_category(metal_detailed, "TO", "polar",       "Metal map: TO polar vs others",           "Metal_TO_polar.pdf")
analyze_aa_category(metal_detailed, "TO", "hydrophobic", "Metal map: TO hydrophobic vs others",     "Metal_TO_hydrophobic.pdf")
analyze_aa_category(metal_detailed, "TO", "proline",     "Metal map: TO proline vs others",         "Metal_TO_proline.pdf")
analyze_aa_category(metal_detailed, "TO", "neg_charged", "Metal map: TO negative charged vs others","Metal_TO_neg_charged.pdf")
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Impact Based on Quaternary Structure}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 11 — Structure-Based Analysis (SASA / Quaternary Domains)
#
# Tests whether positional median DMS scores differ between structural contexts:
#   • core          : buried (SASA < 25 Å²), excluding functional sites
#   • surface       : exposed (SASA > 50 Å²), excluding functional sites
#   • active site   : catalytic residues
#   • dimer interface: residues at the CPOX homodimer interface
#   • MTS           : mitochondrial targeting sequence (residues 1–110)
#
# Pairwise Wilcoxon tests are performed for both baseline and metal maps.
# Results are visualised as boxplots and a triangular heatmap (baseline =
# upper-left triangle, metal = lower-right triangle).
# =============================================================================

library(data.table); library(dplyr); library(stringr); library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2); library(ggpubr)

# SASA sheet: row 1 = description, row 2 = column headers (read_sheet handles this).
# Columns: AA residue | AA position | Total SASA (Å²) | Total relative SASA (%) |
#          Main-chain SASA (Å²) | Main-chain relative SASA (%) |
#          Side-chain SASA (Å²) | Side-chain relative SASA (%) |
#          Polar SASA (Å²) | Polar relative SASA (%) |
#          Apolar SASA (Å²) | Apolar relative SASA (%)
freesasa_data <- read_sheet("SASA") %>%
  transmute(
    from_aa = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`AA position`)),
    sasa    = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`Total SASA (Å²)`))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(from_aa), !is.na(sasa))

# Load position lists for excluded functional regions
ignore_positions <- list(
  AS  = read_sheet("Active Site Residues")$'AA position',
  DI  = read_sheet("Interface Residues")$'AA position',
  MTS = 1:110
)

# Core: buried residues not in any functional region
core_pos <- freesasa_data %>%
  filter(sasa < 25, !from_aa %in% unlist(ignore_positions)) %>%
  pull(from_aa)

# Surface: exposed residues not in any functional region
surface_pos <- freesasa_data %>%
  filter(sasa > 50, !from_aa %in% unlist(ignore_positions)) %>%
  pull(from_aa)

# Load map and annotate each position with its structural context
load_map <- function(file, label) {
  sheet <- c(baseline = "Baseline Map", metal = "Mercury Map")[label]
  read_sheet(sheet) %>%
    mutate(pos = as.numeric(str_extract(hgvs_pro, "\\d+"))) %>%
    group_by(pos) %>%
    summarise(median_score = median(score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(
      residue_type = case_when(
        pos %in% surface_pos          ~ "surface",
        pos %in% core_pos             ~ "core",
        pos %in% ignore_positions$DI  ~ "dimer interface",
        pos %in% ignore_positions$MTS ~ "MTS",
        pos %in% ignore_positions$AS  ~ "active site",
        TRUE                          ~ NA_character_
      ),
      map_label = label
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(residue_type))
}

baseline_res <- load_map("~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx", "baseline")
metal_res    <- load_map("~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",   "metal")

combined_res  <- bind_rows(baseline_res, metal_res) %>%
  mutate(map_type = tolower(map_label))

residue_levels <- c("active site","core","dimer interface","MTS","surface")
combined_res$residue_type <- factor(combined_res$residue_type, levels = residue_levels)

# All pairwise group combinations for Wilcoxon tests
all_combinations <- expand.grid(
  group1 = unique(combined_res$residue_type),
  group2 = unique(combined_res$residue_type),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Perform Wilcoxon for both maps across all pairs
perform_wilcoxon_test <- function(data, combos) {
  nested <- data %>%
    group_by(map_label, residue_type) %>%
    summarise(scores = list(median_score), med = median(median_score, na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = map_label, values_from = c(scores, med), names_sep = "_")
  
  combos %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      p_value_baseline = wilcox.test(
        nested %>% filter(residue_type == group1) %>% pull(scores_baseline) %>% .[[1]],
        nested %>% filter(residue_type == group2) %>% pull(scores_baseline) %>% .[[1]],
        exact = FALSE)$p.value,
      p_value_metal = wilcox.test(
        nested %>% filter(residue_type == group1) %>% pull(scores_metal) %>% .[[1]],
        nested %>% filter(residue_type == group2) %>% pull(scores_metal) %>% .[[1]],
        exact = FALSE)$p.value,
      diff_med_baseline = (nested %>% filter(residue_type == group1) %>% pull(med_baseline)) -
                          (nested %>% filter(residue_type == group2) %>% pull(med_baseline)),
      diff_med_metal    = (nested %>% filter(residue_type == group1) %>% pull(med_metal)) -
                          (nested %>% filter(residue_type == group2) %>% pull(med_metal))
    ) %>% ungroup()
}

wilcoxon_results <- perform_wilcoxon_test(combined_res, all_combinations)

# Convert p-values to ordered significance levels (0–3)
annotate_significance <- function(df, p_col, map_label, diff_col) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[p_col]])) %>%
    mutate(
      significance = case_when(
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.001 ~ "p < 0.001",
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.01  ~ "p < 0.01",
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.05  ~ "p < 0.05",
        TRUE ~ "ns"
      ),
      significance_num = case_when(
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.001 ~ 3,
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.01  ~ 2,
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.05  ~ 1,
        TRUE ~ 0
      ),
      map_type    = map_label,
      diff_median = .data[[diff_col]]
    ) %>%
    select(group1, group2, map_type, p_value = all_of(p_col), significance, significance_num, diff_median)
}

wilcoxon_results_combined <- bind_rows(
  annotate_significance(wilcoxon_results, "p_value_baseline", "baseline", "diff_med_baseline"),
  annotate_significance(wilcoxon_results, "p_value_metal",    "metal",    "diff_med_metal")
)

# SASA histogram with core/surface cut-off lines
p_hist <- ggplot(freesasa_data, aes(x = sasa)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(25, 50), color = c("magenta","green"), linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "freeSASA", x = "SASA (Å²)", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
print(p_hist)
pdf("~/Desktop/freeSASA_histogram.pdf", width = 7, height = 6); print(p_hist); dev.off()

# Significance comparisons to annotate on boxplot
sig_comps <- list(
  c("surface", "core"),
  c("surface", "active site"),
  c("core", "dimer interface")
)

ylim_max <- max(combined_res$median_score, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.75

# Combined boxplot (baseline and metal side by side per structural category)
p_combined <- ggplot(combined_res, aes(x = residue_type, y = median_score, fill = map_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("baseline" = "#11EE89", "metal" = "#EE1176")) +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = sig_comps, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, ylim_max)) +
  labs(x = "Residue Type", y = "Functional Score (Median)", fill = "Map Type") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
print(p_combined)
pdf("~/Desktop/Combined_Baseline_Metal_Boxplot.pdf", width = 8, height = 6); print(p_combined); dev.off()

# Separate boxplots per map
for (map in c("baseline", "metal")) {
  df_map     <- combined_res %>% filter(map_label == map)
  fill_color <- ifelse(map == "baseline", "#11EE89", "#EE1176")
  p <- ggplot(df_map, aes(x = residue_type, y = median_score)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, fill = fill_color) +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE, limits = residue_levels) +
    stat_compare_means(comparisons = sig_comps, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, ylim_max)) +
    labs(title = paste0(str_to_title(map), " Map"),
         x = "Residue Type", y = "Functional Score (Median)") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
  pdf(paste0("~/Desktop/", str_to_title(map), "_Map_Boxplot.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)
  print(p); dev.off()
}

# ── Triangular heatmap ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Each cell is split diagonally: upper-left = baseline, lower-right = metal.
# Fill encodes significance level (white → light pink → red → dark red).
heatmap_matrix <- wilcoxon_results_combined %>%
  select(group1, group2, map_type, significance_num) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = map_type, values_from = significance_num, values_fill = 0) %>%
  rename(residue1 = group1, residue2 = group2) %>%
  mutate(x = factor(residue1, levels = residue_levels),
         y = factor(residue2, levels = residue_levels))

heatmap_matrix$x_num <- as.numeric(heatmap_matrix$x)
heatmap_matrix$y_num <- as.numeric(heatmap_matrix$y)

# Build triangle polygon coordinates for each half of each cell
make_triangles <- function(data, type = c("baseline","metal")) {
  type   <- match.arg(type)
  offset <- if (type == "baseline") list(x = c(-0.5, 0.5,-0.5), y = c( 0.5, 0.5,-0.5))
            else                    list(x = c( 0.5,-0.5, 0.5), y = c(-0.5,-0.5, 0.5))
  data[rep(1:nrow(data), each = 3), ] %>%
    mutate(
      x    = rep(data$x_num, each = 3) + rep(offset$x, times = nrow(data)),
      y    = rep(data$y_num, each = 3) + rep(offset$y, times = nrow(data)),
      fill = rep(data[[type]], each = 3)
    ) %>%
    select(x, y, fill, residue1, residue2)
}

baseline_triangles <- make_triangles(heatmap_matrix, "baseline")
metal_triangles    <- make_triangles(heatmap_matrix, "metal")

p_heatmap <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(data = metal_triangles,
               aes(x = x, y = y, group = interaction(residue1, residue2), fill = factor(fill)),
               color = "black") +
  geom_polygon(data = baseline_triangles,
               aes(x = x, y = y, group = interaction(residue1, residue2), fill = factor(fill)),
               color = "black") +
  coord_fixed() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("0"="white","1"="#FFDDDD","2"="#FF8888","3"="#B20000"),
    breaks = c("0","1","2","3"),
    labels = c("Not Sign.","< 0.05","< 0.01","< 0.001"),
    name   = "Significance (p.value)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq_along(residue_levels), labels = residue_levels,
                     expand = expansion(add = 0.5), position = "top") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq_along(residue_levels), labels = residue_levels,
                     expand = expansion(add = 0.5), trans = "reverse") +
  labs(title = "Triangular Heatmap for Metal and Baseline") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0),
        axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 1),
        panel.grid = element_blank())
print(p_heatmap)
pdf("~/Desktop/Triangular_Heatmap_Baseline_Metal.pdf", width = 8, height = 8)
print(p_heatmap); dev.off()
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Baseline and Metal: Impact Based on Quaternary Position: MTS}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 12 — MTS Region Structure Analysis
#
# Focuses specifically on the mitochondrial targeting sequence (MTS, residues
# 1–110) and its sub-features:
#   • TOM20 motif   : residues 12, 14, 15 — receptor binding
#   • Amphipathic helix: 75–93
#   • MPP cleavage motif: 36–40 (matrix processing peptidase recognition)
#   • Mature N-termini:  109–111
#   • Surface / dimer interface from SASA (excluded from MTS-specific analysis)
#
# Tests whether these MTS sub-features show distinct functional score
# distributions compared to surface positions.
# =============================================================================

# SASA sheet: row 1 = description, row 2 = column headers (read_sheet handles this).
freesasa_data <- read_sheet("SASA") %>%
  transmute(
    from_aa = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`AA position`)),
    sasa    = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`Total SASA (Å²)`))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(from_aa), !is.na(sasa))

ignore_positions <- list(
  AS  = read_sheet("Active Site Residues")$'AA position',  # was $AA — column is "AA position"
  DI  = read_sheet("Interface Residues")$'AA position',    # was $AA — column is "AA position"
  MTS = 1:110
)

surface_positions <- freesasa_data %>%
  filter(sasa > 50, !from_aa %in% unlist(ignore_positions)) %>%
  pull(from_aa)

# Load map and extract position, WT amino acid, and median score per position
load_and_process_map <- function(file, sheet, map_type) {
  read_sheet(sheet) %>%
    mutate(
      pos        = as.numeric(gsub("p\\.([A-Za-z]+)([0-9]+)([A-Za-z]+)", "\\2", hgvs_pro)),
      mutated_aa = gsub("p\\.([A-Za-z]+)([0-9]+)([A-Za-z]+)", "\\1", hgvs_pro),
      map_type   = map_type
    ) %>%
    group_by(pos) %>%
    mutate(median_score = median(score, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    select(pos, mutated_aa, map_type, median_score)
}

baseline_data <- load_and_process_map("~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx", "Baseline Map", "baseline")
metal_data    <- load_and_process_map("~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",   "Mercury Map",  "metal")

combined_data <- bind_rows(baseline_data, metal_data) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  filter(!is.na(pos) & !is.na(mutated_aa) & !is.na(median_score))

# Define MTS sub-feature position sets
TOM20motif_positions  <- c(12, 14, 15)
amphipathicHelix      <- 75:93
Ntermini_positions    <- 109:111
MPP_positions         <- 36:40

# Annotate positions with MTS sub-feature labels
residue_data <- combined_data %>%
  mutate(
    residue_type = case_when(
      pos %in% surface_positions        ~ "surface",
      pos %in% ignore_positions$DI      ~ "dimer interface",
      pos %in% TOM20motif_positions     ~ "TOM20",
      pos %in% MPP_positions            ~ "MPP motif",
      pos %in% amphipathicHelix         ~ "helix",
      pos %in% Ntermini_positions       ~ "N-termini",
      TRUE                              ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(residue_type)) %>%
  mutate(residue_type = factor(residue_type,
                               levels = c("surface","dimer interface","TOM20","MPP motif","helix","N-termini")))

MTS_boxplot <- ggplot(residue_data, aes(x = residue_type, y = median_score, fill = map_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
    "surface"         = "surface",
    "dimer interface" = "dimer interface",
    "TOM20"           = "TOM20",
    "MPP motif"       = "MPP motif",
    "helix"           = "α-helix",
    "N-termini"       = "Mature \nN-termini"
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("baseline" = "#11EE89", "metal" = "#EE1176")) +
  labs(x = "residue type/pos", y = "functional score (median)") +
  theme_minimal()

pdf("~/Desktop/MTS_boxplots.pdf", width = 8, height = 8); print(MTS_boxplot); dev.off()
print(MTS_boxplot)

# Pairwise Wilcoxon tests for MTS sub-features
all_combinations <- expand.grid(
  group1 = unique(residue_data$residue_type),
  group2 = unique(residue_data$residue_type),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

perform_wilcoxon_test <- function(residue_data, all_combinations) {
  nested_data <- residue_data %>%
    group_by(map_type, residue_type) %>%
    summarise(scores = list(median_score), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = map_type, values_from = scores)
  
  wilcoxon_if_possible <- function(x, y) {
    if (length(x) >= 2 && length(y) >= 2) wilcox.test(x, y, exact = FALSE)$p.value else NA
  }
  
  all_combinations %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      p_value_baseline = {
        x <- nested_data %>% filter(residue_type == group1) %>% pull(baseline) %>% .[[1]]
        y <- nested_data %>% filter(residue_type == group2) %>% pull(baseline) %>% .[[1]]
        wilcoxon_if_possible(x %||% numeric(0), y %||% numeric(0))
      },
      p_value_metal = {
        x <- nested_data %>% filter(residue_type == group1) %>% pull(metal) %>% .[[1]]
        y <- nested_data %>% filter(residue_type == group2) %>% pull(metal) %>% .[[1]]
        wilcoxon_if_possible(x %||% numeric(0), y %||% numeric(0))
      }
    ) %>% ungroup()
}

wilcoxon_results <- perform_wilcoxon_test(residue_data, all_combinations)

annotate_significance <- function(df, p_col, map_label) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[p_col]])) %>%
    mutate(
      significance = case_when(
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.001 ~ "p < 0.001",
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.01  ~ "p < 0.01",
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.05  ~ "p < 0.05",
        TRUE ~ "ns"
      ),
      significance_num = case_when(
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.001 ~ 3,
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.01  ~ 2,
        .data[[p_col]] < 0.05  ~ 1,
        TRUE ~ 0
      ),
      map_type = map_label
    ) %>%
    select(group1, group2, map_type, p_value = all_of(p_col), significance, significance_num)
}

wilcoxon_results_combined <- bind_rows(
  annotate_significance(wilcoxon_results, "p_value_baseline", "baseline"),
  annotate_significance(wilcoxon_results, "p_value_metal",    "metal")
)

# Reshape for triangular heatmap
heatmap_matrix <- wilcoxon_results_combined %>%
  select(group1, group2, map_type, significance_num) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = map_type, values_from = significance_num, values_fill = 0) %>%
  rename(residue1 = group1, residue2 = group2) %>%
  mutate(x = factor(residue1), y = factor(residue2))

heatmap_matrix$x_num <- as.numeric(heatmap_matrix$x)
heatmap_matrix$y_num <- as.numeric(heatmap_matrix$y)

make_triangles <- function(data, type = c("baseline","metal")) {
  type   <- match.arg(type)
  offset <- if (type == "baseline") list(x = c(-0.5, 0.5,-0.5), y = c( 0.5, 0.5,-0.5))
            else                    list(x = c( 0.5,-0.5, 0.5), y = c(-0.5,-0.5, 0.5))
  data[rep(1:nrow(data), each = 3), ] %>%
    mutate(
      x    = rep(data$x_num, each = 3) + rep(offset$x, times = nrow(data)),
      y    = rep(data$y_num, each = 3) + rep(offset$y, times = nrow(data)),
      fill = rep(data[[type]], each = 3)
    ) %>%
    select(x, y, fill, residue1, residue2)
}

baseline_triangles <- make_triangles(heatmap_matrix, "baseline")
metal_triangles    <- make_triangles(heatmap_matrix, "metal")
baseline_triangles$fill <- as.numeric(baseline_triangles$fill)
metal_triangles$fill    <- as.numeric(metal_triangles$fill)

residue_levels <- factor(
  unique(c(as.character(baseline_triangles$residue1), as.character(metal_triangles$residue1))),
  levels = c("surface","dimer interface","TOM20","MPP motif","helix","N-termini")
)

baseline_triangles <- baseline_triangles %>%
  mutate(residue1 = factor(residue1, levels = residue_levels),
         residue2 = factor(residue2, levels = residue_levels))
metal_triangles <- metal_triangles %>%
  mutate(residue1 = factor(residue1, levels = residue_levels),
         residue2 = factor(residue2, levels = residue_levels))

MTS_Heatmap <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(data = metal_triangles,
               aes(x = x, y = y, group = interaction(residue1, residue2), fill = factor(fill)),
               color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  geom_polygon(data = baseline_triangles,
               aes(x = x, y = y, group = interaction(residue1, residue2), fill = factor(fill)),
               color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  coord_fixed() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("0"="white","1"="#FFDDDD","2"="#FF8888","3"="#B20000"),
    breaks = c("0","1","2","3"),
    labels = c("Not Sign.","< 0.05","< 0.01","< 0.001"),
    name   = "Significance (p.value)"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = levels(residue_levels), labels = levels(residue_levels)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = levels(residue_levels), labels = levels(residue_levels)) +
  geom_text(data = data.frame(x = levels(residue_levels),
                               y = rep(-2, length(levels(residue_levels)))),
            aes(x = x, y = y, label = x), size = 4, color = "black", angle = 90, hjust = 0) +
  geom_text(data = data.frame(x = rep(-2, length(levels(residue_levels))),
                               y = levels(residue_levels)),
            aes(x = x, y = y, label = y), size = 4, color = "black", angle = 0, hjust = 0) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.key = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black")) +
  labs(title = "Triangular Heatmap for Metal and Baseline",
       x = "Residue Type", y = "Residue Type", fill = "Significance")

pdf("~/Desktop/Heatmap_MTS.pdf", width = 8, height = 8); print(MTS_Heatmap); dev.off()
print(MTS_Heatmap)

# Print pairwise comparisons of specific groups for both maps
print_comparison <- function(data, map_label, group1, group2) {
  scores1 <- data %>% filter(map_type == map_label, residue_type == group1) %>% pull(median_score)
  scores2 <- data %>% filter(map_type == map_label, residue_type == group2) %>% pull(median_score)
  cat(sprintf("\n%s map - %s group size: %d\n", map_label, group1, length(scores1)))
  cat(sprintf("%s map - %s group size: %d\n", map_label, group2, length(scores2)))
  median_diff <- median(scores1, na.rm = TRUE) - median(scores2, na.rm = TRUE)
  wilcox_res  <- wilcox.test(scores1, scores2, exact = FALSE)
  cat(sprintf("%s map comparison: %s vs %s\n", toupper(map_label), group1, group2))
  cat(sprintf("Difference in median functional scores: %.4f\n", median_diff))
  cat(sprintf("Wilcoxon test p-value: %.4g\n", wilcox_res$p.value))
}

print_comparison(residue_data, "baseline", "helix", "surface")
print_comparison(residue_data, "metal",    "helix", "surface")
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Substitution Resulting in Negative Charge for Second Decarboxylation Residues}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 13 — Negative-Charge Substitutions at Second Decarboxylation Residues
#
# CPOX catalyses two sequential decarboxylation reactions. Residues involved
# in each reaction occupy distinct spatial positions:
#   set1 (400–404): second decarboxylation substrate-binding residues
```
