```{r, warning=FALSE, Substitution Resulting in Negative Charge for Second Decarboxylation Residues}
#   set2 (407, 411, 413, 416): second set
#
# This chunk tests whether substitutions TO negatively charged amino acids
# (Asp/Glu) at these specific residue sets are significantly more disruptive
# than:
#   1) All other substitutions at other positions (regardless of AA type)
#   2) TO negative-charge substitutions at all other positions
#
# One-tailed Wilcoxon (alternative = "less") tests whether target scores
# are lower than the comparison group.
# =============================================================================

library(data.table); library(stringr); library(ggplot2); library(ggpubr)

baseline <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
setDT(baseline)
metal    <- read_sheet("Mercury Map")
setDT(metal)

aa_cats_detailed <- list(
  hydrophobic = c("Ala","Gly","Val","Leu","Ile","Met","Phe","Trp","Tyr"),
  polar       = c("Ser","Thr","Asn","Gln","Cys"),
  neg_charged = c("Asp","Glu"),
  pos_charged = c("Arg","Lys","His"),
  proline     = c("Pro")
)

# Lookup table: map each AA to its physicochemical class
aa_lookup <- rbindlist(
  lapply(names(aa_cats_detailed), function(cat) {
    data.table(changeTo = aa_cats_detailed[[cat]], aa_type_To_simple = cat)
  })
)

set1 <- 400:404      # first decarboxylation residue set
set2 <- c(407, 411, 413, 416)  # second set

# Main analysis function: two comparisons per dataset × target set
#   Comparison 1: neg-charged @ target vs. all other substitutions
#   Comparison 2: neg-charged @ target vs. neg-charged at all other positions
process_neg_target_vs_all <- function(df, target_set, exclude_set,
                                       dataset_name = "dataset",
                                       pdf_file_prefix = NULL) {
  setDT(df)
  df[, pos      := as.numeric(str_extract(hgvs_pro, "\\d+"))]
  df[, changeTo := str_extract(hgvs_pro, "(?<=\\d)[A-Z][a-z]{2}$")]
  df <- merge(df, aa_lookup, by = "changeTo", all.x = TRUE)
  
  # Comparison 1: target neg-charged vs. all other (any type, any position)
  neg_target_all <- df[pos %in% target_set & aa_type_To_simple == "neg_charged"]
  all_others_all <- df[!(pos %in% target_set & aa_type_To_simple == "neg_charged") &
                         !(pos %in% exclude_set & aa_type_To_simple == "neg_charged")]
  
  data_all <- rbind(copy(neg_target_all)[, group := "neg_target"],
                    copy(all_others_all)[, group := "all_other"])
  data_all[, group := factor(group, levels = c("neg_target","all_other"))]
  
  medians_all    <- data_all[, .(median_score = median(score, na.rm = TRUE)), by = group]
  median_diff_all <- medians_all[group == "neg_target", median_score] -
                     medians_all[group == "all_other",  median_score]
  test_all <- wilcox.test(score ~ group, data = data_all, alternative = "less", exact = FALSE)
  
  if (!is.null(pdf_file_prefix)) {
    p_all <- ggplot(data_all, aes(x = group, y = score)) +
      geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3) +
      labs(title = paste0(dataset_name, ": neg-charged @ ",
                          paste(target_set, collapse = ","), " vs all other residues"),
           x = "Group", y = "Variant functional score") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
      stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif")
    ggsave(paste0(pdf_file_prefix, "_all_substitutions.pdf"), p_all, width = 6, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Comparison 2: target neg-charged vs. neg-charged at all other positions
  neg_target_neg <- df[pos %in% target_set & aa_type_To_simple == "neg_charged"]
  neg_other_neg  <- df[pos %notin% target_set & aa_type_To_simple == "neg_charged"]
  
  data_neg <- rbind(copy(neg_target_neg)[, group := "neg_target"],
                    copy(neg_other_neg)[,  group := "neg_other"])
  data_neg[, group := factor(group, levels = c("neg_target","neg_other"))]
  
  medians_neg    <- data_neg[, .(median_score = median(score, na.rm = TRUE)), by = group]
  median_diff_neg <- medians_neg[group == "neg_target", median_score] -
                     medians_neg[group == "neg_other",  median_score]
  test_neg <- wilcox.test(score ~ group, data = data_neg, alternative = "less", exact = FALSE)
  
  if (!is.null(pdf_file_prefix)) {
    p_neg <- ggplot(data_neg, aes(x = group, y = score)) +
      geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3) +
      labs(title = paste0(dataset_name, ": neg-charged @ ",
                          paste(target_set, collapse = ","), " vs all other TO negative"),
           x = "Group", y = "Variant functional score") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
      stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif")
    ggsave(paste0(pdf_file_prefix, "_neg_only.pdf"), p_neg, width = 6, height = 6)
  }
  
  list(dataset = dataset_name, target = paste(target_set, collapse = ","),
       median_diff_all = median_diff_all, p_value_all = test_all$p.value,
       median_diff_neg = median_diff_neg, p_value_neg = test_neg$p.value)
}

res1 <- process_neg_target_vs_all(baseline, set1, set2, "baseline_set1", "~/Desktop/baseline_set1")
res2 <- process_neg_target_vs_all(baseline, set2, set1, "baseline_set2", "~/Desktop/baseline_set2")
res3 <- process_neg_target_vs_all(metal,    set1, set2, "metal_set1",    "~/Desktop/metal_set1")
res4 <- process_neg_target_vs_all(metal,    set2, set1, "metal_set2",    "~/Desktop/metal_set2")

for (r in list(res1, res2, res3, res4)) {
  cat("\nDataset:", r$dataset,
      "\nTarget positions:", r$target,
      "\nMedian difference (vs all other substitutions):", round(r$median_diff_all, 4),
      "\nOne-tailed Wilcoxon p (vs all other substitutions):", signif(r$p_value_all, 4),
      "\nMedian difference (vs all other TO negative):", round(r$median_diff_neg, 4),
      "\nOne-tailed Wilcoxon p (vs all other TO negative):", signif(r$p_value_neg, 4),
      "\n----------------------------------------------\n")
}
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Impact of AA substitution and Substrate Preference with Hg}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 14 — Delta Score Impact Classification (APR / Improved / Known)
#
# Classifies variants by their response to mercury (Hg) exposure using
# delta scores (baseline – metal):
#
#   APR (activity reduced in presence of Hg):
#     • Significant delta (p < threshold)
#     • Negative deltaScore (function lost under metal)
#     • Tolerated at baseline (lower CI ≥ 0.5)
#
#   Improved (Hg-rescued):
#     • Significant delta
#     • Positive deltaScore (function gained under metal)
#     • Tolerated at baseline
#
#   Known (no significant delta):
#     • Not significant but tolerated at baseline
#
#   Neutral: all other variants
#
# Produces violin + boxplot distributions with APR variants highlighted,
# relative to a null band derived from synonymous + nonsense delta scores.
# =============================================================================

delta <- read_sheet("Delta Map")
setDT(delta)
delta <- delta[is.finite(deltaScore) & is.finite(deltaSE)]

# Classify variant types
delta[, variant_type  := "Missense"]
delta[grepl("Ter$", hgvs_pro), variant_type := "Nonsense"]
delta[grepl("=$",   hgvs_pro), variant_type := "Synonymous"]
delta[, variant_group := variant_type]
delta[variant_type %in% c("Synonymous","Nonsense"), variant_group := "Synonymous + Nonsense"]

# Load baseline scores and define tolerance threshold
baseline_map <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
setDT(baseline_map)
setnames(baseline_map, colnames(baseline_map)[1:4], c("hgvs_pro","baseline_score","se","df"))
baseline_map[, baseline_lower := baseline_score + se]

baseline_miss          <- !grepl("Ter$|=$", baseline_map$hgvs_pro)
baseline_miss_tolerated <- baseline_map[baseline_miss & baseline_lower >= 0.5, hgvs_pro]

# Assign impact categories
delta[, Impact := "neutral"]
APR_set      <- delta[significant == TRUE  & deltaScore < 0 & hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated, hgvs_pro]
Improved_set <- delta[significant == TRUE  & deltaScore > 0 & hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated, hgvs_pro]
Known_set    <- delta[significant == FALSE & hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated, hgvs_pro]

delta[hgvs_pro %in% APR_set,      Impact := "APR"]
delta[hgvs_pro %in% Improved_set, Impact := "Improved"]
delta[hgvs_pro %in% Known_set,    Impact := "Known"]

# Export impact classification for PyMOL structural colouring
color <- delta[, .(hgvs_pro, Impact)]
color[, score := NA_real_]
color[Impact == "Improved", score := 2]
color[Impact == "neutral",  score := 1.1]
color[Impact == "APR",      score := 0.6]
color[Impact == "Known",    score := 0]
color[, se  := 0]
color[, pos := as.numeric(str_extract(hgvs_pro, "[0-9]+"))]
write.csv(color[, .(hgvs_pro, score, se)], "~/Desktop/CPOX_delta_structure.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Null distribution: synonymous + nonsense delta scores
null_variants <- delta[variant_group == "Synonymous + Nonsense"]
null_median   <- median(null_variants$deltaScore, na.rm = TRUE)
null_se       <- sd(null_variants$deltaScore, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(nrow(null_variants))
null_lower    <- null_median - 1.96 * null_se
null_upper    <- null_median + 1.96 * null_se

# Violin + boxplot with APR variants highlighted as red points
p <- ggplot(delta, aes(x = variant_group, y = deltaScore)) +
  geom_violin(data = subset(delta, variant_group != "Missense"),
              fill = "grey85", color = "grey40", width = 0.9) +
  geom_violin(data = subset(delta, variant_group == "Missense"),
              fill = "lightblue", color = "grey40", width = 0.9) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA, fill = "white") +
  # Shaded null band (95% CI of synonymous + nonsense median)
  geom_rect(
    data = data.frame(xmin = 0.5, xmax = 2.5, ymin = null_lower, ymax = null_upper),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
    fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = null_median, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,           linetype = "dotted") +
  # APR variants as filled red circles
  geom_point(
    data = subset(delta, variant_group == "Missense"),
    aes(fill = Impact == "APR"), shape = 21, color = "black", size = 2.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "white", "TRUE" = "red"), guide = "none") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Delta functional score (baseline – mercury)",
       title = "Delta score distributions with APR variants highlighted") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12))
print(p)

pdf("~/Desktop/APRdistribution.pdf", width = 8, height = 6); print(p); dev.off()

# Summary statistics
total_baseline_miss <- length(baseline_miss_tolerated)
num_APR             <- sum(delta$Impact == "APR", na.rm = TRUE)
percent_APR         <- 100 * num_APR / total_baseline_miss
total_missense      <- sum(delta$variant_type == "Missense")
num_negative        <- sum(delta$deltaScore < 0 & delta$variant_type == "Missense", na.rm = TRUE)

cat(sprintf("APR variants (baseline tolerated, significant negative delta): %d / %d (%.2f%%)\n",
            num_APR, total_baseline_miss, percent_APR))
cat(sprintf("Percentage of missense variants with negative deltaScore: %.2f%%\n",
            100 * num_negative / total_missense))

# Wilcoxon test: all missense delta scores vs. null (synonymous + nonsense)
missense_scores <- delta[variant_type == "Missense", deltaScore]
null_scores     <- delta[variant_group == "Synonymous + Nonsense", deltaScore]
wilcox_all_vs_null <- wilcox.test(missense_scores, null_scores, alternative = "two.sided")

cat(sprintf("Median deltaScore (Missense): %.3f\n", median(missense_scores, na.rm = TRUE)))
cat(sprintf("Median deltaScore (Null: Synonymous + Nonsense): %.3f\n", median(null_scores, na.rm = TRUE)))
print(wilcox_all_vs_null)
```

```{r, warning=FALSE, SNV Accessibility and Mercury Impact}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 14b — SNV Accessibility and Mercury Impact
#
# This analysis determines:
# 1. Which amino acid substitutions are SNV-reachable (single nucleotide 
#    variant accessible based on the genetic code)
# 2. Of those SNV-reachable substitutions, what fraction show statistically 
#    significant mercury effect
# Output: Percentage of SNV-accessible substitutions with mercury impact
# Data source: "Delta Map" sheet from Manuscript_Data.xlsx
# =============================================================================
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PART 1: Build SNV Reachability Matrix
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The genetic code: all codons and their amino acids

genetic_code <- list(
  "TTT" = "Phe", "TTC" = "Phe", "TTA" = "Leu", "TTG" = "Leu",
  "TCT" = "Ser", "TCC" = "Ser", "TCA" = "Ser", "TCG" = "Ser",
  "TAT" = "Tyr", "TAC" = "Tyr", "TAA" = "STOP", "TAG" = "STOP",
  "TGT" = "Cys", "TGC" = "Cys", "TGA" = "STOP", "TGG" = "Trp",
  "CTT" = "Leu", "CTC" = "Leu", "CTA" = "Leu", "CTG" = "Leu",
  "CCT" = "Pro", "CCC" = "Pro", "CCA" = "Pro", "CCG" = "Pro",
  "CAT" = "His", "CAC" = "His", "CAA" = "Gln", "CAG" = "Gln",
  "CGT" = "Arg", "CGC" = "Arg", "CGA" = "Arg", "CGG" = "Arg",
  "ATT" = "Ile", "ATC" = "Ile", "ATA" = "Ile", "ATG" = "Met",
  "ACT" = "Thr", "ACC" = "Thr", "ACA" = "Thr", "ACG" = "Thr",
  "AAT" = "Asn", "AAC" = "Asn", "AAA" = "Lys", "AAG" = "Lys",
  "AGT" = "Ser", "AGC" = "Ser", "AGA" = "Arg", "AGG" = "Arg",
  "GTT" = "Val", "GTC" = "Val", "GTA" = "Val", "GTG" = "Val",
  "GCT" = "Ala", "GCC" = "Ala", "GCA" = "Ala", "GCG" = "Ala",
  "GAT" = "Asp", "GAC" = "Asp", "GAA" = "Glu", "GAG" = "Glu",
  "GGT" = "Gly", "GGC" = "Gly", "GGA" = "Gly", "GGG" = "Gly"
)

# Function: Check if two codons differ by exactly 1 nucleotide
codons_differ_by_one <- function(codon1, codon2) {
  if (nchar(codon1) != 3 || nchar(codon2) != 3) return(FALSE)
  
  diffs <- 0
  for (pos in 1:3) {
    if (substr(codon1, pos, pos) != substr(codon2, pos, pos)) {
      diffs <- diffs + 1
    }
  }
  
  return(diffs == 1)
}

# Function: Check if an amino acid pair is SNV-reachable
# Input: from_aa, to_aa (3-letter codes, e.g., "Ala", "Arg")
# Output: TRUE if ANY codon pair differs by exactly 1 nucleotide
is_snv_reachable_pair <- function(from_aa, to_aa) {
  
  # Get all codons for FROM and TO amino acids
  from_codons <- names(genetic_code[which(genetic_code == from_aa)])
  to_codons <- names(genetic_code[which(genetic_code == to_aa)])
  
  # If either amino acid doesn't exist in genetic code, return FALSE
  if (length(from_codons) == 0 || length(to_codons) == 0) {
    return(FALSE)
  }
  
  # Test every FROM codon against every TO codon
  # If ANY pair differs by exactly 1 nucleotide, return TRUE
  for (from_codon in from_codons) {
    for (to_codon in to_codons) {
      if (codons_differ_by_one(from_codon, to_codon)) {
        return(TRUE)
      }
    }
  }
  
  # No SNV path found between any codon pairs
  return(FALSE)
}

# Pre-compute SNV reachability for all amino acid pairs
# This lookup table lets us quickly determine if any given substitution
# can be created with a single nucleotide change

cat("Building SNV reachability matrix...\n")

snv_reachable_pairs <- data.frame()

all_aa <- unique(unlist(genetic_code))
all_aa <- all_aa[all_aa != "STOP"]

# Test every FROM → TO pair (20 × 19 = 380 combinations)
for (from_aa in all_aa) {
  for (to_aa in all_aa) {
    if (from_aa != to_aa) {
      is_reachable <- is_snv_reachable_pair(from_aa, to_aa)
      snv_reachable_pairs <- rbind(snv_reachable_pairs, data.frame(
        from_aa = from_aa,
        to_aa = to_aa,
        is_snv_reachable = is_reachable,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
}

# Summary of reachability
reachable_count <- sum(snv_reachable_pairs$is_snv_reachable)
cat(sprintf("SNV Reachability Matrix Built:\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Total amino acid pairs tested: %d\n", nrow(snv_reachable_pairs)))
cat(sprintf("  SNV-reachable pairs: %d (%.1f%%)\n", reachable_count,
            100 * reachable_count / nrow(snv_reachable_pairs)))
cat("\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PART 2: Load Delta Data from Excel
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Read the "Delta Map" sheet from Manuscript_Data.xlsx
# This sheet contains all variants with their environmental impact scores
# (significant column) and delta scores

delta_complete <- read_excel("~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx", 
                              sheet = "Delta Map",
                              skip = 1) %>%  # Skip the description row
  as.data.table()
setDT(delta_complete)

# Ensure numeric columns are actually numeric
delta_complete[, `:=`(
  deltaScore = as.numeric(deltaScore),
  deltaSE = as.numeric(deltaSE),
  significant = as.logical(significant)
)]

cat(sprintf("Loaded Delta Map sheet: %d rows\n", nrow(delta_complete)))
cat("\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PART 3: Parse HGVS Notation to Extract Amino Acids
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# HGVS format: p.XxxnnYyy
#   Xxx = FROM amino acid (3-letter code)
#   nn = position number
#   Yyy = TO amino acid (3-letter code, or Ter for nonsense, or = for synonymous)
#
# Example: p.Ala110Arg means Alanine at position 110 → Arginine

delta_complete[, `:=`(
  from_aa = NA_character_,
  to_aa = NA_character_,
  position = NA_integer_,
  is_synonymous = FALSE,
  is_nonsense = FALSE
)]

# Function to parse HGVS and extract amino acid info
parse_hgvs_protein <- function(hgvs_str) {
  # Handle synonymous (p.Ala110=)
  if (grepl("=$", hgvs_str)) {
    from_aa <- regmatches(hgvs_str, regexpr("[A-Z][a-z]{2}", hgvs_str))
    pos <- regmatches(hgvs_str, regexpr("\\d+", hgvs_str))
    return(list(from = from_aa, to = from_aa, pos = as.numeric(pos), 
                is_syn = TRUE, is_stop = FALSE))
  }
  
  # Handle nonsense (p.Ala110Ter)
  if (grepl("Ter$", hgvs_str)) {
    from_aa <- regmatches(hgvs_str, regexpr("[A-Z][a-z]{2}(?=\\d)", hgvs_str, perl = TRUE))
    pos <- regmatches(hgvs_str, regexpr("\\d+(?=[A-Z])", hgvs_str, perl = TRUE))
    return(list(from = from_aa, to = "Ter", pos = as.numeric(pos),
                is_syn = FALSE, is_stop = TRUE))
  }
  
  # Handle missense (p.Ala110Arg)
  from_aa <- regmatches(hgvs_str, regexpr("[A-Z][a-z]{2}(?=\\d)", hgvs_str, perl = TRUE))
  pos <- regmatches(hgvs_str, regexpr("\\d+(?=[A-Z])", hgvs_str, perl = TRUE))
  to_aa <- regmatches(hgvs_str, regexpr("[A-Z][a-z]{2}$", hgvs_str, perl = TRUE))
  
  return(list(from = from_aa, to = to_aa, pos = as.numeric(pos),
              is_syn = FALSE, is_stop = FALSE))
}

# Apply parsing to all variants
for (i in 1:nrow(delta_complete)) {
  parsed <- parse_hgvs_protein(delta_complete[i, hgvs_pro])
  delta_complete[i, `:=`(
    from_aa = parsed$from,
    to_aa = parsed$to,
    position = parsed$pos,
    is_synonymous = parsed$is_syn,
    is_nonsense = parsed$is_stop
  )]
}

cat(sprintf("Parsed HGVS notation for all %d variants\n", nrow(delta_complete)))
cat("\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PART 4: Classify SNV Reachability
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# For each missense variant:
#   1. Extract FROM and TO amino acids
#   2. Look up in SNV reachability table
#   3. Mark as SNV-reachable if ANY codon pair could differ by 1 SNV

delta_complete[, is_snv_reachable := FALSE]

for (i in 1:nrow(delta_complete)) {
  from_aa <- delta_complete[i, from_aa]
  to_aa <- delta_complete[i, to_aa]
  
  # Only classify missense variants (not synonymous or nonsense)
  if (!is.na(from_aa) && !is.na(to_aa) && 
      from_aa != to_aa && 
      to_aa != "Ter" &&
      !delta_complete[i, is_synonymous] &&
      !delta_complete[i, is_nonsense]) {
    
    # Look up reachability in pre-computed table
    match <- snv_reachable_pairs[snv_reachable_pairs$from_aa == from_aa &
                                   snv_reachable_pairs$to_aa == to_aa, ]
    
    if (nrow(match) > 0 && match$is_snv_reachable[1]) {
      delta_complete[i, is_snv_reachable := TRUE]
    }
  }
}

cat("SNV reachability classified for all variants\n")
cat("\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PART 5: Calculate Environmental Impact Statistics
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Count total SNV-reachable missense variants
total_snv_reachable <- sum(delta_complete[, is_snv_reachable], na.rm = TRUE)

# Count SNV-reachable variants with SIGNIFICANT environmental impact
# (significant == TRUE means mercury effect is statistically significant)
snv_with_significant_impact <- sum(
  delta_complete[is_snv_reachable == TRUE & significant == TRUE, .N]
)

# Calculate percentage
pct_snv_with_impact <- if (total_snv_reachable > 0) {
  100 * snv_with_significant_impact / total_snv_reachable
} else {
  NA_real_
}

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PART 6: Print Summary Statistics
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("=================================================================\n")
cat("SNV ACCESSIBILITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ANALYSIS\n")
cat("=================================================================\n\n")

# Overall variant counts
total_variants <- nrow(delta_complete)
missense_count <- sum(!delta_complete[, is_synonymous] & 
                      !delta_complete[, is_nonsense], na.rm = TRUE)
synonymous_count <- sum(delta_complete[, is_synonymous], na.rm = TRUE)
nonsense_count <- sum(delta_complete[, is_nonsense], na.rm = TRUE)

cat("VARIANT TYPE DISTRIBUTION:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Total variants in delta data: %d\n", total_variants))
cat(sprintf("  Missense variants: %d (%.1f%%)\n", missense_count, 
            100 * missense_count / total_variants))
cat(sprintf("  Synonymous variants: %d (%.1f%%)\n", synonymous_count, 
            100 * synonymous_count / total_variants))
cat(sprintf("  Nonsense variants: %d (%.1f%%)\n", nonsense_count, 
            100 * nonsense_count / total_variants))
cat("\n")

# SNV reachability statistics
cat("SNV REACHABILITY (among missense variants):\n")
cat(sprintf("  SNV-reachable substitutions: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            total_snv_reachable, missense_count,
            100 * total_snv_reachable / missense_count))
cat(sprintf("  Non-SNV-reachable substitutions: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            missense_count - total_snv_reachable, missense_count,
            100 * (missense_count - total_snv_reachable) / missense_count))
cat("\n")

# Environmental impact among SNV-reachable
cat("KEY RESULT: ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT (MERCURY EFFECT)\n")
cat("Among SNV-reachable substitutions:\n")
cat(sprintf("  With significant environmental impact: %d / %d (%.2f%%)\n",
            snv_with_significant_impact, total_snv_reachable, pct_snv_with_impact))
cat(sprintf("  Without significant impact: %d / %d (%.2f%%)\n",
            total_snv_reachable - snv_with_significant_impact, total_snv_reachable,
            100 - pct_snv_with_impact))
cat("\n")

# Breakdown by delta score direction
snv_negative_delta <- sum(
  delta_complete[is_snv_reachable == TRUE & significant == TRUE & deltaScore < 0, .N],
  na.rm = TRUE
)
snv_positive_delta <- sum(
  delta_complete[is_snv_reachable == TRUE & significant == TRUE & deltaScore > 0, .N],
  na.rm = TRUE
)

if (snv_with_significant_impact > 0) {
  cat("DIRECTIONALITY (among SNV-reachable with impact):\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Negative delta (reduced function in Hg): %d (%.1f%%)\n",
              snv_negative_delta, 100 * snv_negative_delta / snv_with_significant_impact))
  cat(sprintf("  Positive delta (improved function in Hg): %d (%.1f%%)\n",
              snv_positive_delta, 100 * snv_positive_delta / snv_with_significant_impact))
  cat("\n")
}

# Summary box
cat("=================================================================\n")
cat(sprintf("SUMMARY:\n"))
cat(sprintf("  %.2f%% of SNV-accessible amino acid substitutions\n", pct_snv_with_impact))
cat(sprintf("  show statistically significant environmental impact (mercury)\n"))
cat("=================================================================\n\n")

# Export detailed results
results_export <- delta_complete[is_snv_reachable == TRUE, 
                                 .(hgvs_pro, from_aa, to_aa, position,
                                   deltaScore, deltaSE, significant)]
results_export <- results_export[order(position, from_aa, to_aa)]

write.csv(results_export, "~/Desktop/CPOX_SNV_Environmental_Impact.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat(sprintf("Detailed results exported to: CPOX_SNV_Environmental_Impact.csv\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Total SNV-reachable rows: %d\n", nrow(results_export)))
```

```{r, warning=FALSE, Delta Significance Heatmap}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 15 — Delta Significance Heatmap (Variant-Level)
#
# Produces a full amino acid × position tile heatmap coloured by variant impact
# category (APR / Improved / Known / neutral / Synonymous), with a stacked
# consensus track above showing the fraction of each category per position.
#
# Y-axis order follows the Kyte-Doolittle hydrophobicity scale (AVLIMFYWRHKDESTNQGCP).
# =============================================================================

delta <- read_sheet("Delta Map")
setDT(delta)
delta <- delta[is.finite(deltaScore) & is.finite(deltaSE)]

delta[, variant_type  := "Missense"]
delta[grepl("Ter$", hgvs_pro), variant_type := "Nonsense"]
delta[grepl("=$",   hgvs_pro), variant_type := "Synonymous"]
delta[, variant_group := variant_type]
delta[variant_type %in% c("Synonymous","Nonsense"), variant_group := "Synonymous + Nonsense"]

baseline_map <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
setDT(baseline_map)
setnames(baseline_map, 1:4, c("hgvs_pro","baseline_score","se","df"))
baseline_map[, baseline_lower := baseline_score + se]
baseline_map[, baseline_stop  := grepl("Ter$", hgvs_pro)]
baseline_map[, baseline_syn   := grepl("=$",   hgvs_pro)]
baseline_map[, baseline_miss  := !(baseline_stop | baseline_syn)]
baseline_miss_tolerated <- baseline_map[baseline_miss & baseline_lower >= 0.5, hgvs_pro]

delta[, Impact := "neutral"]
APR_set      <- delta[significant == TRUE  & deltaScore < 0 & hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated, hgvs_pro]
known_set    <- delta[significant == FALSE & hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated, hgvs_pro]
improved_set <- delta[significant == TRUE  & deltaScore > 0 & hgvs_pro %in% baseline_miss_tolerated, hgvs_pro]
delta[hgvs_pro %in% known_set,    Impact := "known"]
delta[hgvs_pro %in% APR_set,      Impact := "APR"]
delta[hgvs_pro %in% improved_set, Impact := "improved"]

# Single-letter amino acid lookup (for y-axis labels)
aa3to1 <- c(
  Ala="A",Arg="R",Asn="N",Asp="D",Cys="C",Gln="Q",Glu="E",Gly="G",
  His="H",Ile="I",Leu="L",Lys="K",Met="M",Phe="F",Pro="P",Ser="S",
  Thr="T",Trp="W",Tyr="Y",Val="V"
)

# Build per-variant heatmap data.frame
heatmap_df <- delta %>%
  mutate(
    pos             = as.numeric(str_extract(hgvs_pro, "[0-9]+")),
    substitution_AA = case_when(
      grepl("=$", hgvs_pro) ~ str_sub(hgvs_pro, 3, 3),          # synonymous: keep original AA
      TRUE                  ~ aa3to1[str_sub(hgvs_pro, -3, -1)]  # missense: extract destination AA
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    color_bin = case_when(
      grepl("=$", hgvs_pro) ~ "Synonymous",
      Impact == "known"     ~ "known",
      Impact == "APR"       ~ "APR",
      Impact == "neutral"   ~ "neutral",
      Impact == "improved"  ~ "improved",
      TRUE                  ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Y-axis order (Kyte-Doolittle hydrophobicity)
aa_order <- str_split("AVLIMFYWRHKDESTNQGCP", "", simplify = TRUE)

plot_df <- heatmap_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(pos) & !is.na(substitution_AA)) %>%
  mutate(
    substitution_AA = factor(substitution_AA, levels = rev(aa_order)),
    color_bin       = factor(color_bin, levels = c("known","APR","neutral","improved","Synonymous"))
  )

# Build stacked consensus track (fractional composition per position)
consensus_df <- heatmap_df %>%
  group_by(pos, color_bin) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(pos) %>%
  mutate(
    frac = count / sum(count),
    ymin = 22 + cumsum(lag(frac, default = 0)),
    ymax = 22 + cumsum(frac)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

impact_colors <- c(
  "known"      = "#AEBBE6",
  "APR"        = "#CDEED6",
  "neutral"    = "#F7F8FD",
  "improved"   = "#DFA6A5",
  "Synonymous" = "#FCED98"
)

heatmap_plot <- ggplot() +
  # Main heatmap tiles
  geom_tile(data = plot_df, aes(x = pos, y = substitution_AA, fill = color_bin),
            width = 1, height = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = impact_colors, na.value = "grey80") +
  # Stacked consensus track above the heatmap (y positions 22+)
  geom_rect(data = consensus_df,
            aes(xmin = pos - 0.5, xmax = pos + 0.5, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = color_bin),
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Amino acid position", y = "Substituted residue",
       title = "CPOX Variant Heatmap with Stacked Consensus Track") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, max(plot_df$pos), by = 5)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 4),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

pdf("~/Desktop/CPOX_heatmap_stacked_consensus.pdf", width = 14, height = 3)
print(heatmap_plot); dev.off()
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Balanced Precision vs Recall Curves for Maps}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 16 — Balanced Precision-Recall Curves (PRC)
#
# Evaluates the clinical discriminative performance of:
#   • CPOX baseline DMS map
#   • CPOX metal DMS map
#   • AlphaMissense
#   • ESM-1v
#   • VARITY_R
#
# Positive class: HCP / HP variants (hereditary coproporphyria / harderoporphyria)
# Negative class: disease_type == "none" (benign)
# VUS and conflicting variants are excluded.
#
# "Balanced" PRC accounts for class imbalance.
# DMS scores are negated before PRC (lower score = more pathogenic in DMS convention).
#
# Four PRC subsets:
#   1. All variants
#   2. Excluding HP (harderoporphyria — atypical severe presentation)
#   3. Excluding MTS positions (< 110) — these may behave differently
#   4. Excluding HCP
#
# A boxplot of scores by clinical annotation (B/LB vs P/LP) is also produced,
# with Wilcoxon test between groups for each map.
# =============================================================================

generate_prc_plots <- function(ref_file, baseline_file, metal_file = NULL) {
  library(data.table); library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(tidyr)
  
  # Load and label reference data
  ref_data <- read_sheet("ReferenceSet") %>%
    mutate(
      Pathogenic = case_when(
        disease_type %in% c("HCP","HP") ~ "pathogenic",
        disease_type == "none"          ~ "benign",
        TRUE                            ~ disease_type
      )
    )
  
  baseline_data <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
  colnames(baseline_data)[2] <- "baseline"
  
  if (!is.null(metal_file)) {
    metal_data <- read_sheet("Mercury Map")
    colnames(metal_data)[2] <- "metal"
  }
  
  complete_dataset <- merge(ref_data, baseline_data, by = "hgvs_pro")
  if (!is.null(metal_file)) {
    complete_dataset <- merge(complete_dataset, metal_data, by = "hgvs_pro")
  } else {
    complete_dataset$metal <- NA
  }
  
  complete_dataset <- complete_dataset %>%
    select(hgvs_pro, baseline, AlphaMissense, ESM.1v, VARITY_R, disease_type, metal) %>%
    mutate(pos = as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9]", "", hgvs_pro)))
  
  # Clinical annotation boxplot
  variant_distribution <- complete_dataset %>%
    mutate(
      color       = ifelse(disease_type == "HP", "red", "grey30"),
      disease_type = case_when(
        disease_type %in% c("HCP","HP") ~ "P/LP",
        disease_type == "none"          ~ "B/LB",
        TRUE                            ~ disease_type
      )
    )
  
  variant_long <- variant_distribution %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(baseline, metal), names_to = "ScoreType", values_to = "Score")
  
  annot_plot <- ggplot(variant_long, aes(x = disease_type, y = Score, fill = ScoreType)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = color, group = ScoreType), size = 2, alpha = 0.8,
                position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.8)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(baseline = "#11EE89", metal = "#EE1176")) +
    scale_color_identity() +
    theme_minimal() +
    xlab("Clinical Annotation") + ylab("Score") +
    labs(title = "Baseline vs Metal Scores by Clinical Annotation", fill = "Map") +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 14))
  print(annot_plot)
  
  # Wilcoxon test: P/LP vs. B/LB for each map
  for (score_type in c("baseline","metal")) {
    group_B_LB <- variant_distribution %>% filter(disease_type == "B/LB") %>% pull(all_of(score_type))
    group_P_LP <- variant_distribution %>% filter(disease_type == "P/LP") %>% pull(all_of(score_type))
    test_result <- wilcox.test(group_P_LP, group_B_LB, exact = FALSE)
    median_diff <- median(group_P_LP, na.rm = TRUE) - median(group_B_LB, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat(sprintf("Score type: %s\nMedian difference (P/LP - B/LB): %.6f\n", score_type, median_diff))
    if (test_result$p.value < 2.2e-16) {
      cat("P-value: < 2.2e-16\n\n")
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("P-value: %.8f\n\n", test_result$p.value))
    }
  }
  
  # Prepare PRC dataset: negate scores (lower DMS score = more pathogenic)
  PRC_data <- complete_dataset %>%
    filter(
      !grepl("VUS",        disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE) &
      !grepl("conflicting",disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      Pathogenic = case_when(
        disease_type %in% c("HCP","HP") ~ TRUE,
        disease_type == "none"          ~ FALSE,
        TRUE                            ~ FALSE
      ),
      baseline = -baseline,
      metal    = ifelse(!is.na(metal), -metal, NA)
    )
  print(PRC_data)
  
  # PRC plotting helper using yogiroc
  plot_prc <- function(data, title) {
    prc_data <- data %>%
      select(Pathogenic, baseline, AlphaMissense, ESM.1v, VARITY_R, metal) %>%
      mutate(Pathogenic = as.logical(Pathogenic))
    
    if (all(is.na(prc_data$metal))) {
      prc_data <- prc_data %>% select(-metal)
      colors   <- c("#11EE89","#D02F43","#2FD0BC","black")
    } else {
      colors   <- c("#11EE89","#D02F43","#2FD0BC","black","#EE1176")
    }
    
    yrobj <- yr2(truth = prc_data$Pathogenic, scores = prc_data[, -1])
    draw.prc(yrobj, balanced = TRUE, main = title, col = colors)
    cat("Total Pathogenic (TRUE) and Benign (FALSE):\n")
    print(table(prc_data$Pathogenic))
  }
  
  # Generate all PRC subsets
  plot_prc(PRC_data, "All Variants")
  plot_prc(PRC_data %>% filter(!grepl("HP",  disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE)), "Excluding HP Variants")
  plot_prc(PRC_data %>% filter(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9]","",hgvs_pro)) >= 110),   "Excluding MTS Positions (2-110)")
  plot_prc(PRC_data %>% filter(!grepl("HCP", disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE)), "Excluding HCP Variants")
  
  # Save PDFs
  pdf("~/Desktop/All_PRC.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
  plot_prc(PRC_data, "All Variants")
  ggsave("~/Desktop/All_ClinicalVariants.pdf", plot = annot_plot, width = 8, height = 6)
  dev.off()
  pdf("~/Desktop/PRC_noHP.pdf",  8, 6)
  plot_prc(PRC_data %>% filter(!grepl("HP",  disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE)), "Excluding HP Variants"); dev.off()
  pdf("~/Desktop/PRC_noHCP.pdf", 8, 6)
  plot_prc(PRC_data %>% filter(!grepl("HCP", disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE)), "Excluding HCP Variants"); dev.off()
  pdf("~/Desktop/PRC_noMTS.pdf", 8, 6)
  plot_prc(PRC_data %>% filter(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9]","",hgvs_pro)) >= 110),   "Excluding MTS Positions (2-110)"); dev.off()
}

generate_prc_plots(
  ref_file      = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",
  baseline_file = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",
  metal_file    = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx"
)
```


```{r, Balanced Precision vs Recall Curve for Random Predictor, warning=FALSE}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 17 — PRC with Random Predictor
#
# Adds a random predictor (shuffled truth labels) to the PRC to provide a
# chance-level baseline. This confirms that the observed PRC area under the
# curve exceeds random performance.
# seed = 123 ensures reproducibility of the random shuffle.
# =============================================================================

library(data.table); library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(tidyr); library(yogiroc)

generate_prc_plots <- function(ref_file, baseline_file, metal_file = NULL) {
  
  ref_data <- read_sheet("ReferenceSet")
  
  baseline_data <- read_sheet("Baseline Map")
  colnames(baseline_data)[2] <- "baseline"
  
  if (!is.null(metal_file)) {
    metal_data <- read_sheet("Mercury Map")
    colnames(metal_data)[2] <- "metal"
  }
  
  complete_dataset <- merge(ref_data, baseline_data, by = "hgvs_pro")
  if (!is.null(metal_file)) {
    complete_dataset <- merge(complete_dataset, metal_data, by = "hgvs_pro")
  } else {
    complete_dataset$metal <- NA
  }
  
  # Prepare PRC data (negate scores, filter VUS/conflicting)
  PRC_data <- complete_dataset %>%
    filter(
      !grepl("VUS",         disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE) &
      !grepl("conflicting", disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      Pathogenic = case_when(
        disease_type %in% c("HCP","HP") ~ TRUE,
        disease_type == "none"          ~ FALSE,
        TRUE                            ~ FALSE
      ),
      baseline = -baseline,
      metal    = ifelse(!is.na(metal), -metal, NA)
    ) %>%
    select(hgvs_pro, Pathogenic, baseline, AlphaMissense, ESM.1v, VARITY_R, metal)
  
  # Add random predictor: shuffled pathogenicity labels (set.seed for reproducibility)
  set.seed(123)
  PRC_data$Random <- -1 * as.numeric(sample(PRC_data$Pathogenic))
  
  cat("\n===== PRC DATA TABLE USED =====\n")
  print(PRC_data)
  cat("\nPathogenic counts:\n")
  print(table(PRC_data$Pathogenic))
  
  score_matrix <- PRC_data %>%
    select(baseline, AlphaMissense, ESM.1v, VARITY_R, metal, Random)
  
  colors <- c(
    "#11EE89",  # baseline
    "#D02F43",  # AlphaMissense
    "#2FD0BC",  # ESM.1v
    "black",    # VARITY_R
    "#EE1176",  # metal
    "grey50"    # Random
  )
  
  yrobj <- yr2(truth = PRC_data$Pathogenic, scores = score_matrix)
  
  pdf("~/Desktop/PRC_Random.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
  draw.prc(yrobj, balanced = TRUE, main = "All Variants (Random Included)", col = colors)
  dev.off()
  
  cat("\nPRC figure saved to ~/Desktop/PRC_Random.pdf\n")
}

generate_prc_plots(
  ref_file      = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",
  baseline_file = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx",
  metal_file    = "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx"
)
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, LLRp}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 18 — LLRp Evidence for VUS Reclassification
#
# Applies the Tavtigian et al. (2020) log-likelihood ratio (LLR) framework
# to quantify functional evidence strength for CPOX ClinVar VUS variants.
#
# LLR thresholds (ACMG/AMP evidence levels):
#   > 1.2723   : pathogenic very strong
#   0.636–1.272: pathogenic moderate
#   0.318–0.636: pathogenic supporting
#  -0.318–0.318: no evidence
#  -1.272–-0.318: benign supporting
#   < -1.272   : benign strong
#
# A stacked bar chart shows the evidence distribution per ClinVar category
# (P/LP, B/BP, VUS). Percentage of VUS with actionable evidence is reported.
# =============================================================================

LLRp_data <- read_sheet("LLRp Scores")

# Assign ACMG evidence categories based on LLR thresholds
LLRp_data$category <- dplyr::case_when(
  LLRp_data$llr >  0.636167   & LLRp_data$llr <  1.2723233 ~ "patho_moderate",
  LLRp_data$llr >  0.3180808  & LLRp_data$llr <  0.636167  ~ "patho_support",
  LLRp_data$llr <  0.3180808  & LLRp_data$llr > -0.3180808 ~ "none",
  LLRp_data$llr > -1.2723233  & LLRp_data$llr < -0.3180808 ~ "benign_support",
  LLRp_data$llr < -1.2723233                                ~ "benign_strong",
  TRUE                                                       ~ "patho_vstrong"
)

# Load and classify reference variants
ref_Set <- read_sheet("ReferenceSet")
ref_Set <- ref_Set %>%
  mutate(
    clinvar = case_when(
      grepl("HP|HCP", disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "P/LP",
      grepl("none",   disease_type, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "B/BP",
      TRUE                                              ~ "VUS"
    )
  )

new_evidence_data <- merge(LLRp_data, ref_Set, by = "hgvs_pro")
new_evidence_data <- new_evidence_data[, c("hgvs_pro","clinvar","llr","category")]

# Colour scheme: green (benign) → grey (none) → purple (pathogenic)
category_colors <- c(
  "benign_strong"  = "#50DD22",
  "benign_support" = "#A0FF80",
  "none"           = "grey50",
  "patho_support"  = "#D18BEE",
  "patho_moderate" = "#AF22DD",
  "patho_strong"   = "#9B00C8",
  "patho_vstrong"  = "#8000A0"
)

LLR_evidence <- ggplot(new_evidence_data, aes(x = clinvar, fill = category)) +
  geom_bar(position = position_stack(reverse = TRUE)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "ClinVar Category", y = "Count", fill = "LLR Category") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave(filename = "~/Desktop/LLR_evidence.pdf", plot = LLR_evidence, width = 8, height = 6)

# Percentage of VUS with any functional evidence (i.e. category ≠ "none")
vus_data          <- new_evidence_data[new_evidence_data$clinvar == "VUS", ]
total_vus         <- nrow(vus_data)
vus_with_none     <- nrow(vus_data[vus_data$category == "none", ])
vus_without_none  <- ((total_vus - vus_with_none) / total_vus) * 100

cat("Percentage of VUS entries without 'none' in category:", vus_without_none, "%\n")
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, VUS Numbers}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 19 — VUS Reclassification Summary
#
# Breaks down the functional evidence categories for the VUS subset.
# Reports counts and percentages (calculated excluding "none" from denominator
# to show the distribution among those with actual evidence).
# =============================================================================

# Counts per category for VUS variants
category_counts <- table(vus_data$category)

# Exclude "none" for percentage calculation (report among actionable evidence only)
categories_for_percentage <- category_counts[names(category_counts) != "none"]

category_percentages <- (categories_for_percentage / sum(categories_for_percentage)) * 100

category_summary <- data.frame(
  Category   = names(categories_for_percentage),
  Count      = as.vector(categories_for_percentage),
  Percentage = as.vector(category_percentages)
)

cat("Counts and Percentages of VUS entries by category (excluding 'none' from percentage):\n")
print(category_summary)
```


```{r, warning=FALSE, Variant Validation}
# =============================================================================
# CHUNK 20 — Variant Validation (Growth Curves)
#
# Analyses yeast growth curves from a 96-well plate reader experiment used
# to orthogonally validate a subset of DMS variants.
#
# Data source: 'Growth Curves Summary' sheet in Manuscript_Data.xlsx
#   Dataset='WT_N272'    : WT vs p.Asn272His across five mercury concentrations
#   Dataset='0 vs 96 µM' : WT, p.Asn272His, p.Leu155Trp, p.Gly188Gln at 0 and 96 µM
#
# Condition and Comparison columns are mercury concentrations in µM.
#
# Output: mean ± SD growth curves (linear and log scale) with ribbon shading.
# =============================================================================

DATA_PATH     <- "~/Desktop/Manuscript_Data.xlsx"
output_prefix <- "~/Desktop/CPOX_Growth_Curves"

# ── Read data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
gc <- read_excel(DATA_PATH, sheet = "Growth Curves Summary", skip = 1) %>%
  mutate(Time_hours = as.numeric(Time_hours))

df_summary <- gc %>%
  filter(Dataset == "WT_N272") %>%
  mutate(
    Variant    = factor(Variant, levels = c("WT", "N272")),
    Condition  = factor(Condition),
    Comparison = as.numeric(Comparison)
  )

df_AD <- gc %>%
  filter(Dataset == "0 vs 96 µM") %>%
  mutate(
    Variant   = factor(Variant, levels = unique(Variant)),
    Condition = factor(Condition)
  )

# ── Loop over comparison concentrations (WT_N272 dataset) ─────────────────
comparison_rows  <- unique(df_summary$Comparison)
plot_list_linear <- list()
plot_list_log    <- list()

for (comparison_conc in comparison_rows) {

  df_plot <- df_summary %>% filter(Comparison == comparison_conc)

  linetype_values <- setNames(c("dotted", "solid"), unique(as.character(df_plot$Condition)))
  color_values    <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(4, "Set1")[1:2]
  plot_key        <- paste0(comparison_conc, "uM")

  p_linear <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(Time_hours, mean_OD, color = Variant, linetype = Condition)) +
    geom_line(size = 1, aes(group = interaction(Variant, Condition))) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_OD - sd_OD, ymax = mean_OD + sd_OD, fill = Variant),
                alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = linetype_values) +
    scale_color_manual(values = color_values) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_values) +
    labs(title = paste0("Growth Curves (Linear) — 0 vs ", comparison_conc, " µM"),
         x = "Time (hours)", y = "OD600") +
    theme_minimal()

  p_log <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(Time_hours, mean_OD, color = Variant, linetype = Condition)) +
    geom_line(size = 1, aes(group = interaction(Variant, Condition))) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_OD - sd_OD, ymax = mean_OD + sd_OD, fill = Variant),
                alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = linetype_values) +
    scale_y_log10() +
    scale_color_manual(values = color_values) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_values) +
    labs(title = paste0("Growth Curves (Log scale) — 0 vs ", comparison_conc, " µM"),
         x = "Time (hours)", y = "OD600 (log scale)") +
    theme_minimal()

  ggsave(paste0(output_prefix, "_", plot_key, "_Linear.pdf"), p_linear, width = 8, height = 6)
  ggsave(paste0(output_prefix, "_", plot_key, "_Log.pdf"),    p_log,    width = 8, height = 6)

  plot_list_linear[[plot_key]] <- p_linear
  plot_list_log[[plot_key]]    <- p_log
}

# ── 0 vs 96 µM plot (all variants) ────────────────────────────────────────
linetype_values_AD <- setNames(c("dotted", "solid"), levels(df_AD$Condition))
color_values_AD    <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(4, "Set1")[1:length(unique(df_AD$Variant))]

p_AD_linear <- ggplot(df_AD, aes(Time_hours, mean_OD, color = Variant, linetype = Condition)) +
  geom_line(size = 1, aes(group = interaction(Variant, Condition))) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_OD - sd_OD, ymax = mean_OD + sd_OD, fill = Variant),
              alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = linetype_values_AD) +
  scale_color_manual(values = color_values_AD) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = color_values_AD) +
  labs(title = "Growth Curves (Linear) — 0 vs 96 µM (All Variants)",
       x = "Time (hours)", y = "OD600") +
  theme_minimal()

p_AD_log <- ggplot(df_AD, aes(Time_hours, mean_OD, color = Variant, linetype = Condition)) +
  geom_line(size = 1, aes(group = interaction(Variant, Condition))) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_OD - sd_OD, ymax = mean_OD + sd_OD, fill = Variant),
              alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = linetype_values_AD) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(values = color_values_AD) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = color_values_AD) +
  labs(title = "Growth Curves (Log scale) — 0 vs 96 µM (All Variants)",
       x = "Time (hours)", y = "OD600 (log scale)") +
  theme_minimal()

plot_list_linear[["0_vs_96uM_all"]] <- p_AD_linear
plot_list_log[["0_vs_96uM_all"]]    <- p_AD_log

# ── Combined grid ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
combined_linear <- wrap_plots(plot_list_linear, ncol = 3)
combined_log    <- wrap_plots(plot_list_log,    ncol = 3)

ggsave(paste0(output_prefix, "_Combined_Linear.pdf"), combined_linear, width = 16, height = 8)
ggsave(paste0(output_prefix, "_Combined_Log.pdf"),    combined_log,    width = 16, height = 8)
```
